title: ♬ P. Leela, ‘Forgotten’ Singer Who Sang Unforgettable  Songs In Tamil Cinema ♥
body_lines: (P. Leela was the uncrowned queen of song in the realm of Tamil films during the fifties and sixties of the last century.Born in 1934, Leela passed away in 2005.This Article written in 2015 is re-posted here without any changes to denote Leela’s Birthdy on May 19)

I was pleasantly surprised this week when a South Indian journal asked me to revise an article I had written earlier about the singer Porayathu Leela – popularly known as P. Leela. The request for an article to denote Leela’s birthday was most unexpected as she had passed away 10 years ago on October 31st 2005.Her  once successful career as a  Play back singer of film songs in Tamil cinema had virtually ended decades before her demise. Leela, born on 19 May 1934, was ethnically a Malayalee who resided and died in the Tamil Nadu state capital of Chennai.

The present generation of film song fans may have never heard her or even heard of P. Leela. She was the uncrowned queen of song in the realm of Tamil films during the fifties and sixties of the last century. P. Leela has sung about 5,000 film songs in South Indian languages such as Tamil, Malayalam, Telugu, Kannada and even in Bengalee and Sinhala. Her Tamil renditions may number around 1,000. She was a household name in those days.

There was a time when Indian artistes and technicians played a big role in Sinhala films. The Sinhala film industry was in its nascent stages and forced to rely on Indian input to a great extent. Many Sinhala films were produced in India. Film songs in Sinhala films were heavily influenced by Hindi and Tamil films. Indian music directors such as V. Dakshinamoorthy, S.M. Subbiah Nayudu and Vedha composed music for Sinhala films. Indian playback singers like Jamuna Rani, K. Rani, S.Janaki ,AM Rajah and Leela used to sing for Sinhala films in those days.

My years of childhood and early teens was an era where Tamil film singers like T.M. Soundararajan, P. Susheela, P.B. Sreenivas, Seergali Govindarajan, S. Janaki and L.R. Eeswari, etc. dominated. Singers like Leela were not actively singing in Tamil films then. Despite this it was etched in my consciousness as a kid that Leela was the best singer of them all. This was due to a rather facetious reason.

The early sixties saw the “Leela stores” cum “Leela press” owner Mr.Sinnathurai bringing out an illustrated calendar in Ceylon as Sri Lanka was known then. An illustrated calendar or ‘Sithirakkalendar’ was a novelty when first introduced. Radio advertisements were used greatly to market the new product. The similarity of the “Leela” name between the singer and calendar was smartly used to promote the product.

Most ads would first have a line from a popular song by Leela like ‘Engume Aanandham’ or ‘Konchum Salangai Oli Kettu’. Thereafter a voice would say that just like Leela’s songs, the Leela calendar too was the best or that like a song by Leela, the namesake calendar too gave pleasure. This made an indelible impression on me that Leela was the best singer at that impressionable age. Of course in later years one listened to more old songs and realised that all those nightingales of yesteryear were unique in their own way.

My mother too was an ardent fan of Leela. Her favourite Tamil film singer duo or ‘jodi’ were Leela and the renowned singer Ghantasala. Leela was a Malayalee and Ghantasala a Telugu. Yet they were a popular pair singing in Tamil  and Telugu films then. Voice wise, Ghantasala was a bass and Leela a soprano but both harmonised well. My mother would often say that the voices of Ghantasala and Leela were made for each other. The duo hit it off perfectly in films like ‘Bhathala Bhairavi,’ ‘Maya Bhajaar’ and ‘Prema Pasam,’ with evergreen numbers like ‘Amaithi Illathen Maname,’ ‘Ennathan un Premaiyo,’ ‘Kathale Deiveega,’ ‘Neethanaa Ennai,’ ‘Aahaa Inba Nilavinile’ and ‘Veesum Thendralile,’ etc.

Apart from Ghantasala, Leela’s voice blended well with male singers like A.M. Rajah, T.M. Soundararajan and Seergali Govindarajan also.Among her many duets with AM Raja were ”Vaarayo Vennilaave” in ”Missiamma”, “odameethirunthe” in “Assai Magan” and “Naan seitha Poojapalam” in “Gunasundari”. Some of her memorable numbers with Soundararajan were “Mugathinil Mugam Parkalam ” (Thangappadumai), ” Nenjil kudiyirukkum” (Irumbuthirai), “Thendral urangidakkoodumadi” (Sangili Thevan), “Thalaiyam Poomudichu” (Bhagapirivinai) , “Sundari Soundari” (Thookku thookki), “Igalogame” (Thangamalai Rahasyam) “Poovindri Manamethu” (Thangappathumai) and “Nilave Nee Intha ” (Pattinathar).Seergali and Leela also hit it off well with items like “Ellaiyillatha Inbathile” (Chakkaravarthi Thirumagal) , “Kuttrala Aruviyile” (Nallavan Valvan) and “Kalathai Matrinan” (Manithan Maravillai).

As stated earlier when I was growing up – on a diet of Tamil films and Tamil film songs – playback singers like Ghantasala, Leela, A.M. Rajah, Jikki, Chidambaram Jeyaraman, M.L. Vasanthakumari, Tiruchi Loganathan, etc., were on the decline in Tamil films. This was not due to any shortcoming on their part. In fact they were at their peak then. Unfortunately they were not given many opportunities to sing in Tamil movies.

This was due to four reasons. One was that particular film music composers and film directors had begun avoiding them for various reasons. Another was that the old custom of having a variety of singers render playback was giving way to a practice of having only two or three singers per film. Thirdly the number of songs in a film too was being progressively reduced. Finally, leading actors and actresses of that time began insisting that only T.M. Soundararajan and P. Susheela should sing for them.

As a result, singers like Leela got only a few chances in Tamil after the early sixties. So the new Tamil films that I saw in those years did not have her songs. But there was always ‘Radio Ceylon’. I also became an ardent film aficionado and sought out old Tamil movies. Leela’s songs were featured in many of those.

Leela’s voice and singing style endeared her to me. It was an inimitable yet sweet amalgam of at least five of her contemporaries – M.L. Vasanthakumari, Jikki, (Radha) Jeyalakshmi, T.S. Bhagavathy and A.P. Komala. It was like a veritable “Panchamirtham” (an Ambrosiac mixture of milk, honey, curd, ghee and fruit juice offered as “prasadam” to the Gods). Leela’s resonant voice always rang out loud and clear whether in a solo, a duet or as part of chorus singing (Koshti Ganam).

Porayathu Leela was born in Chithoor near Palghat, Kerala on May 19 1934. Her early years were in Kochi where her father Kunchan Menon was a school master. He was devoted to Carnatic music, even encouraging Leela’s mother Meenakshi Kutti to learn Carnatic after marriage. The father recognised the musical ability and voice range of his daughter at a very early age. He arranged for music lessons and as a child Leela was made to practice alone between 4-6 a.m. daily.

Chennai or Madras was the South Indian capital during the Madras presidency days of the British Raj. It was the cultural Mecca for those of Dravidian linguistic heritage. Realising his daughter’s potential needed a change of place, Kunchan Menon resigned his job in 1944 and re-located to Madras. Leela continued her music tutelage under Rama Bhagavathar along with Pathamadai Krishna Aiyar and Maruthuvakkudi Rajagopal Aiyer in Madras. The father and daughter were also present for all Carnatic song events by reputed exponents.

Leela made her mark when she came first at the ‘Ragam-Thalam-Pallavi’ competition organised by the Madras Viswath Sabha in 1944. The judges were G.N. Balasubramaniam and Palghat Mani Aiyer. This gave her a break when the Andhra Mahila Samithi arranged for regular performances. She began making a name as an up-and-coming Carnatic musician. Neither the father nor daughter ever wanted to sing in films at that time.

The forties however saw a new phenomenon known as playback singing in Indian cinema. Instead of actors singing their songs they were merely lip-synching the words on screen. Regular singers were singing the songs. The final sound track was made after combining both through re-recording. Many professional singers were earning money and fame by singing in films.

The music composer Pathmanabha Shastri heard Leela sing and was impressed. An offer was made. Father and daughter were reluctant first. They were sceptical about the new medium. Finally they agreed. Leela made her entry into playback singing for cinema in 1948. It was the Tamil film ‘Kankanam’ starring K.R. Ramasamy and Menaka. Leela sat on the floor and sang into the mike. Four of her songs were recorded for the film. She never looked back after that.

1949 marked the turning point in her career. She began music lessons under musical prodigy C.R. Subbaraman (CRS), who was also scoring music for films. CRS gave chances to his pupil. Leela sang first for CRS in the Kannada film ‘Bhaktha Kabeer’ in 1949. CRS also gave opportunities to Leela in the Tamil movies ‘Mohini’ and ‘Velaikkari’. ‘Aahaa Ivar Yaaradi’ sung for ‘Mohini’ with K.V. Janaki and ‘Oridanthanile’ for ‘Velaikkari’ were hits. In the 1950s Leela began to sing in all South Indian films. A singing star was born.

It was also Subbaraman who enabled Leela to impress connoisseurs and commoners alike with a classic number. It was for the film ‘Manamagal’ and a duet for a dance by the ‘Travancore sisters,’ Lalitha and Padmini. CRS wanted Leela to sing with the legendary singer M.L. Vasanthakumari. Leela was nervous to sing along but was persuaded by her mentor. It was the ragamalika (medley) ‘Ellam Inba Mayam’.

MLV and Leela moved effortlessly from raga to raga off-screen even as Lalitha and Padmini danced in rhythmic movement on-screen. Leela held her own with MLV and established her name as a singer par excellence of classical melody. Incidentally MLV and Leela were the only two playback singers of that generation with formal training in Carnatic music.

Leela was a singer who could sing almost any type of song at any pace. Classical, semi classical, light and folk songs were all her forte. Devotional, dancing, gypsy, folk, romantic songs were all part of her repertoire. She could grasp the melody easily and sing with all intonations. She kept the ‘shruthi’ low and sang out lustily, giving full range to her mellifluous voice.

Leela was a simple and humble person without any airs or tantrums. She cooperated with all her music directors and co-singers well. Leela has sung for many actresses like Padmini, Savithri, Jamuna, Anjali Devi, T.R. Rajakumari, Rajasulosana, Vijayakumari and even Saroja Devi. It is felt that her voice suited Padmini and Savithri best. She has also sung for composers like C.R. Subbaraman, Ghantasala, K.V. Mahadevan, S.M. Subbiah Naidu, Rajeswara Rao, C. Ramachandra, G. Ramanathan and Viswanathan-Ramamoorthy. Almost every composer elicited the best from her, though CRS, Ghantasala, KVM and G. Ramanathan excelled in utilising her talent.
There are innumerable solos in Tamil films by Leela. In my opinion the finest four of her solos were in ‘Missiamma,’ ‘Vanchikottai Vaaliban’ and ‘Thangappathumai’.

‘Missiamma’ starred Gemini Ganesh and real-life wife Savithri. The two remarkable Leela solos were ‘Mayame Naan Ariven’ and ‘Enaiyalum Mary Matha’. The latter an appeal to the Virgin Mary was sung on an emotional high though Leela is a devout Hindu. It is on par with the Jikki-Periyanayaki number on Mary in ‘Gnanasoundari’ – ‘Arul Tharum Theva Mathave’.

The solo from ‘Vanchikottai Valiban’ is ‘Vennilave Thanmathiye Ennudane Va Va’. It is a soothing melody set on screen for Padmini in a nocturnal boat scene. The melody is evocative of the gentle breeze, moonlight glow, ripples in the water and above all the slow, rhythmic movement of the boat. It transports one to an ethereal atmosphere.

The fourth solo is the sad number ‘Sollamma Vai Thiranthu’ in ‘Thangappathumai’. It is played out by Padmini on screen. The music is by Viswanathan-Ramamoorthy. Leela has gone on record saying that it was the most difficult song she has ever sung for Thamizh films. The recording took place from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. at a 12-hour stretch. Since it was full of pathos, Leela was emotionally high-strung. Viswanathan was not easily satisfied and made Leela labour ceaselessly. At the end of it all Leela wept uncontrollably. Leela’s singing plus Padmini’s acting made the song sequence a great success.

Even if I consider the above four as the four best solos of Leela, the one that I like most  of her solos is the song ‘Then Suvai Mevum Senthamil Geetham’ in ‘Dr. Savithri’. The musical score is by maestro G. Ramanathan, who usually gave ample scope to Leela in the movies he worked in. The song is based on Eastern and Western classical music. The song is picturised on actress Anjali Devi and “Veena”maestro S. Balachander who plays the piano himself in the scene.

Leela sings in sopranic tones to piano accompaniment. The piano interludes are slight variations of a classical movement. Leela begins with an ‘Aaahaara bhirugha’ of incredible sweetness and follows up with similar interspersals. The words too extol the virtues of music and song. She sings ‘Aanandha Geetham’ (A Song of Happiness) is an “Amudha Saagaram’ (Ocean of Ambrosia). How true! The song is almost like a mission statement signed by Leela.

Among other popular and well – appreciated solos are “Kathiruppan Kamalakkannan” in “Uthama Puthiran” where Leela sings a ragamalika set by Ramanathan for a Bharatham sequence by Padmini and her younger sibling Ragini. Leela moves from one raga to another smoothly. So remarkable is the singing that many mistake the singer for MLV. Another of Leelas clasical gems is “Neeye Kathi Eeswari”set to Sarukesi raga in “Annayin Aanai”. The composer is SM Subbaiah Naidu who also appreciated Leela’s work greatly.

It was Subbiah who composed music for “Konchum Salangai” for which Leela sings the theme song “Konchum Salangai Oli Kettu, Nenjil Ponguthamma Puthiya Paattu”. The hit song of the film however was “Singara Velane Deva”. It was this song – accompanied by Karukurichi Arunasalam’s nathaswaram – that launched S. Janaki’s career. What is interesting is that Subbiah Naidu wanted Leela to sing it initially.

Leela however declined saying she could not do full justice to the song and recommended the relatively unknown Janaki as most suitable to sing it. A rare act of frankness and magnanimity!. When Janaki performed in Toronto some years ago, she recollected this incident and expressed her gratitude to” Leelaamma.”

Leela has also sung several duets with singers of her own sex. The songstress combinations have been successes very often. It was the “inbamayam” duet with MLV in “Manamagal” that made Leela’s name. There have been other duets with others too like “Vetkamaga irukkuthadi” – Soolamangalam Rajalakshmi in “Paar Magakey Paar” and “Vaaliya Needooli” – (Radha) Jayalakshmi in “Arivaali”. Both were bharatanatyam numbers. Leela however clicked marvellously with Jikki and P. Susheela.

The crowning achievement with Jikki was in “Vanchikottai Vaaliban” by Gemini Studios. Music was by C. Ramachandra of “Anarkali” fame. Two great danseuse actresses Padmini and Vyjayanthimala were the heroines vying for hero Gemini Ganesans affection. There is this magnificient dance competition between both in the presence of the hero and villain Veerappa. Jikki sings for Vyjayanthi and Leela for Padmini in the classic number “Kannum Kannum Kalanthu”. The song written by Kothamangalam Subbu is a verbal duel. The words, melody, dancing and singing prove to be an excellent combination. At one point Veerappa exclaims “Sabash! Sariyana Potti” (wow! Great competition). It was not only apt for the on screen dancing but also applicable for the off – screen singing by Jikki and Leela.

Leela’s much praised duet with Susheela was in “Lavakusha” starring NT Rama Rao and Anjali Devi. The duet was a marathon 12 minute number “Jegam Pugalum Punniya Kathai Ramanin Kasthaiye, Athai Sevi kulira Padiduvom Kelungal Ithaiye”. The words of Maruthakasi encapsule the story of Ramayana in that song. The music is by Ghantasala. Leela and Susheela sing it in Telugu also in the Telugu version of the film.Sri Sathya Sai Baba was greatly enamoured of that song. He referred to Leela and Susheela as Lava and Kusha because it was Lava and Kusa who sing it on screen.
It was indeed a pity that a songstress of Leela’s calibre was slowly sent into oblivion by Tamil cinema. Leela was not one to canvass for songs and allowed herself to fade away gracefully.

Leela sang in a Tamil film in 1970 after a gap of many years. The film was ‘Engirundho Vanthal,’ where a skit on Sakunthalai was performed on screen. The song by Seergali Govindarajan and Leela begins as ‘Kalithasa Maha Kavi Kaviyam, Kanni Sakunthalai Endroru Oviyam’. It was poignantly appropriate that Leela voiced for Sakunthalai. Like Dushyanthan in Kalidas’s epic forgetting Sakunthalai, the Tamil film world too had forgotten Leela.

After ‘Engiruntho Vandhal’ in 1970 there was a long lull till Ilaiyarajah got her to sing in ‘Katpoora Mullai’ in 1984. It was for a classical song by Sri Vidya on screen. The actress was the daughter of M.L. Vasanthakumari with whom Leela has sung together in many films.

Apart from Tamil cinema even the orthodox music societies of Tamil Nadu ignored Leela. She was a formally trained Carnatic singer but the ‘Sabhas’ of Chennai did not give her many assignments, treating her as a second class playback singer. Kerala and Andhra gave her many stage programs. She was however given continuous prominence by Malayalam radio and television.

Leela’s marriage to a lawyer was a failure. She lived with her sister and family in a Chennai suburb until her demise in 2005 at the age of 71. Former film star Jayalalitha Jayaram who was then Tamil Nadu Chief Minister summed up Leela’s life aptly in her condolence message. Jayalalitha described Leela as “one of the greatest vocal musicians of India, who made a name for herself by singing exquisite songs in her mellifluous voice in Malayalam, Tamil and Telugu both for the film industry, as well as in Carnatic music.”

The Tamil tinsel world and snobbish Sabhas may have rejected Leela, but thousands of her fans never spurned her. Whenever a program featuring her was staged in Tamil Nadu or abroad, many flocked to see and hear her. Unfortunately, such occasions were few. Technology however enables her fans to listen again and again to the melodious voice at a mere click.
Tamil cinema may have forgotten Leela, but her songs remain unforgettable. The name P. Leela can never be erased from the sphere of Tamil film songs.
This article written for the “Spotlight” Column appears in the “Daily FT” of May 23, 2015. It can be accessed here:
P. Leela, ‘forgotten’ singer who sang unforgettable  songs in Tamil Cinema

si.title: ♬ දෙමළ සිනමාවේ අමතක නොවන ගීත ගැයූ ‘අමතක වූ’ ගායිකාව පී.ලීලා ♥
body_lines: (පසුගිය ශතවර්ෂයේ පනස් හැට ගණන්වල දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ක්ෂේත්‍රයේ ඔටුනු නොපළන ගීත රැජින පී. ලීලා ය. 1934 දී උපත ලැබූ ලීලා 2005 දී අභාවප්‍රාප්ත විය. 2015 දී ලියන ලද මෙම ලිපිය කිසිදු වෙනසක් නොමැතිව මෙහි නැවත පළ කෙරේ. මැයි 19 වෙනිදා ලීලාගේ උපන්දිනය දක්වන්න)

P. Leela නමින් ජනප්‍රිය ගායන ශිල්පියෙකු වන පොරයාතු ලීලා ගැන මා කලින් ලියූ ලිපියක් සංශෝධනය කරන ලෙස දකුණු ඉන්දීය සඟරාවක් මගෙන් ඉල්ලා සිටීමත් සමඟ මේ සතියේ මා පුදුමයට පත් විය. ලීලාගේ උපන්දිනය දැක්වීමට ලිපියක් ඉල්ලා සිටීම අනපේක්ෂිත වූයේ ඇය මීට වසර 10 කට පෙර 2005 ඔක්තෝම්බර් 31 වෙනිදා අභාවප්‍රාප්ත වූ බැවිනි. දමිළ සිනමාවේ චිත්‍රපට ගී පසුබිම් ගායිකාවක් ලෙස කලක් සාර්ථක වූ ඇයගේ වෘත්තීය ජීවිතය ඇගේ අභාවයට දශක ගණනාවකට පෙර පාහේ අවසන් වී තිබුණි. 1934 මැයි 19 වන දින උපත ලද ලීලා, තමිල්නාඩු ප්‍රාන්තයේ අගනුවර වන චෙන්නායි හි පදිංචිව සිට මිය ගිය වාර්ගික වශයෙන් මලයාලි ජාතිකයෙකි.

වර්තමාන චිත්‍රපට ගීත රසික පරපුර ඇයව කිසිදාක අසා නැතුව ඇති. පී.ලීලා ගැන අසාවත් ඇති. ඇය පසුගිය ශතවර්ෂයේ පනස් ගණන්වල සහ හැට ගණන්වල දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ක්ෂේත්‍රයේ ඔටුනු නොපළන ගීත රැජින විය. පී.ලීලා දකුණු ඉන්දීය භාෂා වන දෙමළ, මලයාලම්, තෙළිඟු, කන්නඩ සහ බෙංගාලි සහ සිංහල යන භාෂාවලින් පවා චිත්‍රපට ගීත 5,000ක් පමණ ගායනා කර ඇත. ඇගේ දමිළ පරිවර්තන 1000ක් පමණ විය හැක. ඇය ඒ දවස්වල ගෙදර නමක්.

ඉන්දියානු ශිල්පීන් සහ කාර්මික ශිල්පීන් සිංහල චිත්‍රපටවල විශාල කාර්යභාරයක් ඉටු කළ යුගයක් තිබුණා. සිංහල චිත්‍රපට කර්මාන්තය එහි ආරම්භක අවධියේ සිටි අතර බොහෝ දුරට ඉන්දියානු ආදානය මත විශ්වාසය තැබීමට බල කෙරුනි. බොහෝ සිංහල චිත්‍රපට ඉන්දියාවේ නිෂ්පාදනය විය. සිංහල චිත්‍රපටවල චිත්‍රපට ගීත හින්දි සහ දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල බලපෑමට ලක් විය. ඉන්දියානු සංගීත අධ්‍යක්ෂවරුන් වන වී. දක්ෂිණමූර්ති, එස්.එම්. සිංහල චිත්‍රපට සඳහා සංගීත නිර්මාණය කළේ සුබයියා නායුදු සහ වේධා. Jamuna Rani, K. Rani, S.Janaki, AM Rajah, Leela වැනි ඉන්දීය පසුබිම් ගායක ගායිකාවන් ඒ දවස්වල සිංහල චිත්‍රපටවලට ගායනා කළා.

මගේ ළමා කාලය සහ නව යොවුන් වියේ මුල් කාලය දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගායක ටී.එම්. සෞන්දරරාජන්, පී.සුශීලා, පී.බී. ශ්‍රීනිවාස්, සීර්ගලී ගෝවින්දරාජන්, එස්.ජානකී සහ එල්.ආර්. ඊශ්වරී ආදීන් ආධිපත්‍යය දැරූහ. ලීලා වැනි ගායිකාවන් එදා දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල ක්‍රියාකාරීව ගායනා කළේ නැහැ. එසේ තිබියදීත්, ඔවුන් අතරින් හොඳම ගායිකාව ලීලා බව කුඩා කාලයේ මගේ සිහිය තුළ තැන්පත් වී තිබුණි. මෙය තරමක් කාරුණික හේතුවක් නිසා විය.

හැටේ දශකයේ මුල් භාගයේ "ලීලා ස්ටෝර්ස්" සහ "ලීලා ප්‍රෙස්" හිමිකරු සින්නතුරෙයි මහතා ලංකාවේ එදා ශ්‍රී ලංකාව හැඳින්වූ පරිදි නිදර්ශන කැලැන්ඩරයක් ගෙන එන ලදී. නිදර්ශන කැලැන්ඩරයක් හෝ 'සිතිරක්කලෙන්දර්' මුලින්ම හඳුන්වා දුන් විට නව්‍යතාවයක් විය. නව නිෂ්පාදනය අලෙවි කිරීම සඳහා ගුවන්විදුලි දැන්වීම් විශාල වශයෙන් භාවිතා කරන ලදී. ගායකයා සහ දින දර්ශනය අතර "ලීලා" නාමයේ සමානකම නිෂ්පාදනය ප්‍රවර්ධනය කිරීම සඳහා දක්ෂ ලෙස භාවිතා කරන ලදී.

බොහෝ දැන්වීම්වල මුලින්ම ලීලාගේ ජනප්‍රිය ගීතයක් වන ‘එංගුමේ ආනන්දම්’ හෝ ‘කොංචුම් සළංගයි ඔලි කෙට්ටු’ වැනි පේළියක් ඇත. ඊට පස්සේ කටහඬකින් කියනවා ලීලාගේ ගීත වගේම ලීලා කැලැන්ඩරයත් හොඳම හෝ ලීලාගේ ගීතයක් වගේ නාමික කැලැන්ඩරයත් සතුටක් ගෙනාවා කියලා. ඒ සිත් ඇදගන්නා වයසේ හොඳම ගායිකාව ලීලා බව මෙය මා තුළ නොමැකෙන හැඟීමක් ඇති කළේය. ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම පසු වසරවලදී, එක් අයෙකු තවත් පැරණි ගීතවලට සවන් දුන් අතර, අතීතයේ සිටි සියලුම නයිටිංගේල් ඔවුන්ගේම ආකාරයෙන් අද්විතීය බව වටහා ගත්තේය.

මගේ අම්මත් ලීලාගේ දැඩි රසිකාවියක්. ඇයගේ ප්‍රියතම දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගායක යුගලය හෙවත් ‘ජෝඩි’ වූයේ ලීලා සහ කීර්තිමත් ගායිකාවක් වන ඝණ්ඨසාලා ය. ලීලා මලයාලි වූ අතර ඝණ්ටසාලා තෙළිඟු ජාතිකයෙකි. එහෙත් ඔවුන් එවකට දෙමළ සහ තෙළිඟු චිත්‍රපටවල ගායනා කළ ජනප්‍රිය යුගලයක් විය. කටහඬ අනුව, ඝණ්ඨසාලා බාස් කෙනෙක් වූ අතර ලීලා සෝප්‍රානෝවක් වූ නමුත් දෙදෙනාම හොඳින් එකඟ විය. ඝණ්ඨසාලාගෙයි ලීලාගෙයි කටහඬ හැදුණේ එකිනෙකාට කියලා අම්මා නිතර කියනවා. 'භතල බහිරවි', 'මායා භජාර්' සහ 'ප්‍රේම පසම්' වැනි චිත්‍රපටවල, 'ඇමයිති ඉල්ලත්තෙන් මනමේ,' 'එන්නතන් උන් ප්‍රේමයියෝ,' 'කතලේ දෙයිවීගා,' 'නීතානා එන්නයි,' වැනි සදාහරිත අංක සහිත චිත්‍රපටවල මේ දෙපළ හොඳින්ම පහර දුන්නා. 'ආආආ ඉන්බ නිලාවිනිලේ' සහ 'වීසුම් තෙන්ද්‍රලීලේ' යනාදිය.

ඝණ්ටසාලා හැරුණු විට ලීලාගේ කටහඬ ඒ.එම්. රාජා, ටී.එම්. සෞන්දරරාජන් සහ සීර්ගලී ගෝවින්දරාජන් ද.ඇය ඒ.එම්.රාජා සමඟ ගායනා කළ යුගල ගීත අතර “මිසියම්මා” හි “වාරායෝ වෙන්නිලාවේ”, “අස්සයි මගන්” හි “ඔඩමීතිරුන්තේ” සහ “ගුණසුන්දරී” හි “නාන් සීත පූජාපාලම්” විය. සෞන්දරරාජන් සමඟ ඇයගේ මතකයේ රැඳුණු අංකවලින් සමහරක් වූයේ “මුගතිනිල් මුගම් පාරකලම්” (තංගප්පදුමයි), “නෙන්ජිල් කුඩිඉරුක්කුම්” (ඉරුඹුතිරෙයි), “තේන්ද්‍රල් උරංගිදක්කුඩුමඩි” (සංගිලි තේවන්), “තලෙයියම් පූමුඩිචු” (භාගපිරිවිනයි) (භාගපිරිවිනයි) , "ඉගලෝගමේ" (තංගමලෙයි රහස්යම්) "පූවින්ද්‍රි මනමෙතු" (තංගප්පතුමායි) සහ "නිලවේ නී ඉන්තා" (පත්තිනාතර්).සීර්ගලී සහ ලීලා ද "එල්ලයිල්ලත ඉන්බතිලේ" (චක්කරවර්ති තිරුමගල්) (චක්කරවර්ති තිරුමගල්) , "කියුත්‍රෑල" , නල්ලවන් වැල්වන්) සහ "කලතයි මාත්‍රිනන්" (මනිතන් මාරවිලයි).

මා හැදී වැඩෙන විට මුලින් ප්‍රකාශ කළ පරිදි - දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සහ දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීතවල ආහාර වේලක් මත - ඝණ්ටසාලා, ලීලා, ඒ.එම්. රාජා, ජික්කි, චිදම්බරම් ජෙයරාමන්, එම්.එල්. වසන්තකුමාරි, තිරුචි ලෝගනාදන් ආදි දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල පසුබෑමකට ලක් විය. මෙය ඔවුන්ගේ කිසිදු අඩුපාඩුවක් නිසා සිදුවූවක් නොවේ. ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම ඔවුන් එදා සිටියේ උච්චතම ස්ථානයේ ය. අවාසනාවකට දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල ගායනයට වැඩි අවස්ථාවක් ඔවුන්ට ලැබුණේ නැහැ.

මෙය හේතු හතරක් නිසා විය. එකක් නම් විශේෂිත චිත්‍රපට සංගීත නිර්මාපකයින් සහ චිත්‍රපට අධ්‍යක්ෂවරුන් විවිධ හේතූන් මත ඒවා මග හැරීමට පටන් ගෙන තිබීමයි. තවත් එකක් නම්, විවිධ ගායක ගායිකාවන් වාදනය කිරීමේ පැරණි චාරිත්‍රය චිත්‍රපටයකට ගායකයින් දෙතුන් දෙනෙකු පමණක් සිටීමේ පුරුද්දකට මග පෑදීමයි. තෙවනුව චිත්‍රපටියක ගීත සංඛ්‍යාවද ක්‍රමයෙන් අඩුවෙමින් පැවතුනි. අන්තිමට ඒ කාලේ හිටපු ප්‍රධාන නළු නිළියෝ ටී.එම්. සෞන්දරරාජන් සහ පී.සුශීලා ඔවුන් වෙනුවෙන් ගායනා කළ යුතුයි.

මේ නිසා ලීලා වැනි ගායිකාවන්ට හැටේ දශකයේ මුල් භාගයෙන් පසු දෙමළෙන් අවස්ථා ලැබුණේ කිහිපයක් පමණි. ඉතින් ඒ අවුරුදුවල මම බලපු අලුත් දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල ඇගේ ගීත තිබුණේ නැහැ. නමුත් හැමදාම තිබුණේ ‘රේඩියෝ සිලෝන්’. මමත් දැඩි චිත්‍රපට රසිකයෙක් වෙලා පැරණි දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සොයා ගියා. ඒවායින් බොහොමයක් ලීලාගේ ගීත ඇතුළත් විය.

ලීලාගේ කටහඬ සහ ගායන විලාසය ඇයව මගේ සිත් ගත්තේය. එය අවම වශයෙන් ඇගේ සමකාලීනයන් පස් දෙනෙකුගේ අසමසම නමුත් මිහිරි සංයෝගයකි - එම්.එල්. වසන්තකුමාරි, ජික්කි, (රාධා) ජෙයලක්ෂ්මි, ටී. භගවතී සහ ඒ.පී.කොමල. එය සැබෑ "පංචාමිර්තම්" (දෙවියන්ට "ප්‍රසාදය" ලෙස පූජා කරන ලද කිරි, මී පැණි, කිරි, ගිතෙල් සහ පළතුරු යුෂ මිශ්‍රණයක් වැනිය. ලීලාගේ අනුනාද කටහඬ සෑම විටම ඝෝෂාකාරීව සහ පැහැදිලිව නාද විය.

පොරයතු ලීලා උපත ලැබුවේ 1934 මැයි 19 වන දින කේරළයේ පල්ගාට් අසල චිත්තූර් හි ය. ඇගේ මුල් කාලය කොචි හි ඇගේ පියා කුංචන් මෙනන් පාසල් ගුරුවරයෙකු විය. ඔහු කර්නාටක සංගීතයට කැප වූ අතර ලීලාගේ මව වන මීනක්ෂි කුට්ටි විවාහයෙන් පසු කර්නාටික ඉගෙනීමට පවා දිරිමත් කළේය. පියා තම දියණියගේ සංගීත හැකියාව සහ හඬ පරාසය ඉතා කුඩා අවධියේදීම හඳුනා ගත්තේය. ඔහු සංගීත පාඩම් සංවිධානය කළ අතර කුඩා කාලයේ ලීලාව දිනපතා අලුයම 4-6 අතර තනිවම පුහුණු කරන ලදී.

බ්‍රිතාන්‍ය රාජ්‍යයේ මදුරාසි ජනාධිපති සමයේ දකුණු ඉන්දීය අගනුවර වූයේ චෙන්නායි හෝ මදුරාසියයි. එය ද්‍රවිඩ භාෂා උරුමයන් සඳහා සංස්කෘතික මක්කම විය. තම දියණියගේ හැකියාව තේරුම් ගත් කුංචන් මෙනන් 1944 දී සිය රැකියාවෙන් ඉල්ලා අස්වී නැවත මදුරාසියේ පදිංචි විය. ලීලා මදුරාසියේ පතාමඩෙයි ක්‍රිෂ්ණ අයියර් සහ මරුතුවක්කුඩි රාජගෝපාල් අයියර් සමඟ රාම භගවතර් යටතේ ඇගේ සංගීත අධ්‍යාපනය දිගටම කරගෙන ගියාය. කීර්තිමත් ගායකයන්ගේ සියලුම කර්නාටක ගීත ඉසව් සඳහා පියා සහ දියණිය ද පැමිණ සිටියහ.

1944 දී මදුරාසි විශ්ව සභාව විසින් සංවිධානය කරන ලද ‘රාගම්-තාලම්-පල්ලවි’ තරගයේ පළමු ස්ථානයට පැමිණි ලීලා තම සලකුණ තැබුවාය. විනිසුරුවන් වූයේ ජී.එන්. බාලසුබ්‍රමනියම් සහ පල්ගාට් මනි අයියර්. ආන්ද්‍ර මහිලා සමිතිය විසින් නිත්‍ය ප්‍රසංග සඳහා කටයුතු සූදානම් කළ විට මෙය ඇයට විවේකයක් ලබා දුන්නේය. ඇය නැගී එන කර්නාටක සංගීතඥයෙකු ලෙස නමක් දිනා ගැනීමට පටන් ගත්තාය. ඒ කාලේ තාත්තාටවත් දුවටවත් චිත්‍රපටවල ගී ගයන්න උවමනාවක් තිබුණේ නැහැ.

හතළිස් ගණන්වල කෙසේ වෙතත් ඉන්දියානු සිනමාවේ පසුගාමී ගායනය ලෙස හැඳින්වෙන නව සංසිද්ධියක් දක්නට ලැබුණි. නළු නිළියන් ඔවුන්ගේ ගීත ගායනා කරනවා වෙනුවට ඔවුන් කළේ තිරයේ ඇති වචන තොල් සමමුහුර්ත කිරීමයි. නිත්‍ය ගායක ගායිකාවන් ගීත ගායනා කළා. නැවත පටිගත කිරීම හරහා දෙකම ඒකාබද්ධ කිරීමෙන් පසු අවසාන ශබ්ද පථය සාදන ලදී. බොහෝ වෘත්තීය ගායක ගායිකාවන් චිත්‍රපටවල ගායනා කරමින් මුදල් හා ප්‍රසිද්ධිය උපයා ගනිමින් සිටියහ.

ගීත රචක පද්මනාභ ශාස්ත්‍රී ලීලාගේ ගායනය අසා සිත් ගත්තේය. යෝජනාවක් ඉදිරිපත් කළා. තාත්තයි දුවයි මුලින්ම අකමැති වුණා. ඔවුන් නව මාධ්‍ය ගැන සැක පහළ කළා. අවසානයේ ඔවුන් එකඟ විය. ලීලා 1948 දී සිනමාව සඳහා පසුබිම් ගායනයට පිවිසියේය. ඒ කේ.ආර්. රඟපෑ ‘කන්කානම්’ දමිළ චිත්‍රපටයයි. රාමසාමි සහ මේනකා. ලීලා බිම ඉඳගෙන මයික් එකට සින්දු කිව්වා. ඇගේ ගීත හතරක් චිත්‍රපටය සඳහා පටිගත කරන ලදී. ඉන් පසු ඇය ආපසු හැරී බැලුවේ නැත.

1949 ඇගේ වෘත්තීය ජීවිතයේ හැරවුම් ලක්ෂය සනිටුහන් කළේය. ඇය චිත්‍රපට සඳහා සංගීතය සපයන C.R. Subbaraman (CRS) යටතේ සංගීත පාඩම් ආරම්භ කළාය. CRS ඔහුගේ ශිෂ්‍යයාට අවස්ථා ලබා දුන්නේය. ලීලා 1949 දී කන්නඩ චිත්‍රපටයක් වන ‘භක්තා කබීර්’ හි සීආර්එස් සඳහා ප්‍රථම වරට ගායනා කළාය. සීආර්එස් විසින් ලීලාට ‘මෝහිනී’ සහ ‘වේලයික්කාරි’ යන දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල ද අවස්ථා ලබා දුන්නේය. ‘මෝහිනී’ වෙනුවෙන් ගැයූ ‘Aahaa Ivar Yaaradi’ සමඟින් K.V. ‘වේලයික්කාරි’ සඳහා ජානකී සහ ‘ඔරිදන්තනිලේ’ ජනප්‍රිය වුණා. 1950 ගණන්වල ලීලා දකුණු ඉන්දීය චිත්‍රපට සියල්ලටම ගායනා කරන්න පටන් ගත්තා. ගායන තරුවක් බිහි විය.

සම්භාව්‍ය සංඛ්‍යාවක් සමඟ රසඥයන් සහ සාමාන්‍ය ජනයා එකසේ විශ්මයට පත් කිරීමට ලීලාට හැකි වූයේ ද සුබරාමන් ය. ඒ ‘මනමාගල්’ චිත්‍රපටිය සඳහා සහ ‘ට්‍රැවන්කූර් සහෝදරියන්’ ලලිතා සහ පද්මිණීගේ නර්තනයක් සඳහා වූ යුගලයක් සඳහා ය. CRS හට අවශ්‍ය වූයේ ලීලා ජනප්‍රිය ගායක M.L සමඟ ගායනා කිරීමටය. වසන්තකුමාරි. ලීලා සමඟ ගායනා කිරීමට කලබල වූ නමුත් ඇගේ උපදේශකයා විසින් ඒත්තු ගැන්වීය. ඒ රාගමලිකා (මිඩ්ලේ) ‘එල්ලම් ඉන්බ මායම්’ ය.

ලලිතා සහ පද්මිනී තිරයේ රිද්මයානුකූලව නටද්දී MLV සහ ලීලා ආයාසයකින් තොරව රාගයෙන් රාගයට තිරයෙන් පිටත ගමන් කළහ. ලීලා MLV සමඟින් ඇයගේම වූ අතර සම්භාව්‍ය තනු නිර්මාණයේ විශිෂ්ටතම ගායිකාවක් ලෙස ඇගේ නම තහවුරු කළාය. කර්නාටක සංගීතය පිළිබඳ විධිමත් පුහුණුවක් ලැබූ එම පරම්පරාවේ එකම පසුබිම් ගායකයින් දෙදෙනා වූයේ MLV සහ ලීලා පමණි.

ලීලා ඕනෑම ආකාරයක ගීතයක් ඕනෑම වේගයකින් ගායනා කළ හැකි ගායිකාවක් විය. සම්භාව්‍ය, අර්ධ සම්භාව්‍ය, සැහැල්ලූ සහ ජන ගී සියල්ල ඇගේ ශක්තිය විය. භක්ති, නැටුම්, ජිප්සීස්, ජන, ආදර ගීත සියල්ලම ඇගේ ප්‍රසංගයේ කොටස් විය. ඇයට තනුව පහසුවෙන් ග්‍රහණය කර ගැනීමටත් සියලු ස්වරයෙන් ගායනා කිරීමටත් හැකි විය. ඇය ‘ශ්‍රුති’ පහත් කර තෘෂ්ණාවෙන් ගායනා කළේ ඇගේ මුදු හඬට සම්පූර්ණ පරාසයක් ලබා දෙමිනි.

ලීලා කිසිම වාතයක්, තරහක් නැති සරල නිහතමානී කෙනෙක්. ඇය ඇගේ සියලුම සංගීත අධ්‍යක්ෂවරුන් සහ සම-ගායකයන් සමඟ හොඳින් සහයෝගයෙන් කටයුතු කළාය. පද්මිණී, සාවිත්‍රි, ජමුනා, අංජලී දේවි, ටී.ආර්.ආදී නිළියන් රැසකට ලීලා ගයා ඇත. රාජකුමාරි, රාජසුලෝසනා, විජයකුමාරි සහ සරෝජා දේවි පවා. ඇයගේ කටහඬ පද්මිණීට සහ සාවිත්‍රිට වඩාත් ගැලපෙන බව හැඟේ. ඇය C.R. Subbaraman, Ghantasala, K.V වැනි තනු නිර්මාපකයන්ටද ගායනා කර ඇත. මහාදේවන්, එස්.එම්. සුබයියා නයිදු, රාජේශ්වර රාඕ, සී. රාමචන්ද්‍ර, ජී. රාමනාදන් සහ විශ්වනාදන්-රාමමූර්ති. CRS, Ghantasala, KVM සහ G. රාමනාදන් ඇගේ දක්ෂතාව උපයෝගී කරගනිමින් විශිෂ්ටත්වය දැක්වූ නමුත් සෑම නිර්මාපකයෙකුම පාහේ ඇයගෙන් හොඳම දේ ලබා ගත්හ.
ලීලාගේ දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල අනන්තවත් ඒකල තියෙනවා. මගේ මතය අනුව ඇයගේ හොඳම ඒකල හතර වූයේ ‘මිසියම්මා’, ‘වංචිකෝට්ටෙයි වලිබන්’ සහ ‘තංගප්පතුමායි’ ය.

‘මිස්සියම්මා’ චිත්‍රපටයේ රඟපෑවේ ජෙමිනි ගනේෂ් සහ සැබෑ ජීවිතයේ බිරිඳ සාවිත්‍රි. කැපී පෙනෙන ලීලා ඒකල දෙක වූයේ ‘මායාමේ නාන් අරිවෙන්’ සහ ‘එනයියාලුම් මේරි මාතා’ ය. ලීලා භක්තිමත් හින්දු භක්තිකයෙකු වුවද, කන්‍යා මරිය තුමියට කරන ආයාචනය හැඟීම්බරව ගායනා කරන ලදී. එය ‘ඥානසෞන්දරී’ – ‘අරුල් තරුම් තේවා මාතාවේ’ හි මේරිගේ ජික්කි-පෙරියනායකී අංකයට සමාන වේ.

‘වංචිකෝට්ටෙයි වලිබන්’ චිත්‍රපටයේ ඒකල ගීතය වන්නේ ‘වෙන්නිලවේ තන්මතියේ එන්නදනේ වා වා’ යන්නයි. එය නිශාචර බෝට්ටු දර්ශනයක පද්මිණී සඳහා තිරය මත සැකසුණු සන්සුන් තනු නිර්මාණයකි. තනුව මෘදු සුළඟ, සඳ එළිය, ජලයේ රැළි සහ සියල්ලටම වඩා බෝට්ටුවේ මන්දගාමී, රිද්මයානුකූල චලනයන් උද්දීපනය කරයි. එය කෙනෙකුව වායුගෝලයට ප්‍රවාහනය කරයි.

හතරවන ඒකපුද්ගලය වන්නේ ‘තංගප්පතුමායි’ හි ‘සොල්ලම්මා වෛ තිරන්තු’ දුක්ඛිත අංකයයි. එය තිරයේ රඟපාන්නේ පද්මිණී විසිනි. සංගීතය විශ්වනාදන්-රාමමූර්තිගෙනි. ලීලා තමිෂ් චිත්‍රපට සඳහා ඇය ගායනා කළ දුෂ්කරම ගීතය බව පවසමින් වාර්තා ගත කර ඇත. පටිගත කිරීම පෙරවරු 9 සිට රාත්‍රී 9 දක්වා සිදු විය. පැය 12 ක දිගකින්. එය ව්‍යාකූලත්වයෙන් පිරී තිබූ බැවින්, ලීලා චිත්තවේගීයව ඉහළ මට්ටමක සිටියේය. විශ්වනාදන් ලෙහෙසියෙන් සෑහීමකට පත් නොවූ අතර ලීලාව නොනවත්වා වැඩ කළේය. සියල්ල අවසානයේ ලීලාට දරාගත නොහැකිව හැඬුවාය. ලීලාගේ ගායනය සහ පද්මිණීගේ රංගනය ගීත මාලාව ඉතා සාර්ථක විය.

ලීලාවේ හොඳම ඒකල හතර ලෙස මා ඉහත හතර සැලකුවද, ඇයගේ ඒකල ගීතවලින් මා වඩාත් ප්‍රිය කරන්නේ ‘ආචාර්‍යවරයාගේ ‘තේන් සුවයි මෙවුම් සෙන්තමිල් ගීතම්’ ගීතයටය. සාවිත්‍රී' සාමාන්‍යයෙන් ඔහු වැඩ කළ චිත්‍රපටවල ලීලාට ප්‍රමාණවත් ඉඩක් ලබා දුන් විශාරද ජී. රාමනාදන් සංගීතවත් කරයි. ගීතය පෙරදිග හා බටහිර ශාස්ත්‍රීය සංගීතය මත පදනම් වේ. මෙම ගීතය නිළි අංජලී දේවි සහ එම දර්ශනයේ පියානෝව වාදනය කරන "වීණා" විශාරද එස්. බාලචන්දර් මත රූපගත කර ඇත.

ලීලා පියානෝ වාදනය සඳහා සොප්‍රානික් නාදවලින් ගායනා කරයි. පියානෝ අතුරුමුහුණත යනු සම්භාව්‍ය චලනයක සුළු වෙනස්කම් වේ. ලීලාව ආරම්භ වන්නේ ඇදහිය නොහැකි මිහිරි බවකින් යුත් 'ආහාර භිරුඝා' සමඟින් වන අතර ඒ හා සමාන අන්තර් විපර්යාසයන් අනුගමනය කරයි. වචන ද සංගීතයේ හා ගීතයේ ගුණ වර්ණනා කරයි. ඇය ගායනා කරන්නේ ‘ආනන්ද ගීතම්’ (සතුටේ ගීතයක්) යනු “අමුද සාගරම්” (ඇම්බ්‍රෝසියා සාගරය) ය. කොච්චර ඇත්තද! ගීතය ලීලා විසින් අත්සන් කරන ලද මෙහෙවර ප්රකාශයක් වැනි ය.

අනෙකුත් ජනප්‍රිය සහ හොඳින් ඇගයීමට ලක් වූ ඒකල ගීත අතර "උත්තම පුතිරන්" හි "කතිරුප්පන් කමලක්කන්නන්" නම් පද්මිණී සහ ඇගේ බාල සොහොයුරිය වන රාගිනී විසින් භාරතම් අනුපිළිවෙලක් සඳහා රාමනාදන් විසින් සකස් කරන ලද රාගමාලිකාවක් ලීලා ගායනා කරයි. ලීලා එක රාගයකින් තවත් රාගයකට සුමටව ගමන් කරයි. ගායනය කෙතරම් විශිෂ්ටද යත් බොහෝ දෙනෙක් ගායකයා MLV ලෙස වරදවා වටහා ගනී. තවත් ලීලාස් සම්භාව්‍ය මැණික් වර්ගයක් වන්නේ “අන්නයින් ආනයි” හි සරුකේසි රාගයට සැකසූ “නීයේ කති ඊශ්වරී” ය. රචනාකරු එස් එම් සුබ්බයියා නායිදු වන අතර ඔහු ලීලාගේ කාර්යය බෙහෙවින් අගය කළේය.

"කොංචුම් සළංගයි" සඳහා ලීලා විසින් "කොන්චුම් සළංගයි ඔලි කෙට්ටු, නැංජිල් පොංගුතම්මා පුතිය පත්තු" තේමා ගීතය ගයන "කොන්චුම් සළංගයි" සඳහා සංගීතය නිර්මාණය කළේ සුබ්බයියා විසිනි. කෙසේ වෙතත් චිත්‍රපටයේ ජනප්‍රිය ගීතය වූයේ "සිංගර වෙලනේ දේවා" ගීතයයි. කරුකුරිච්චි අරුණාසලම්ගේ නාථස්වරම් සමඟින් ගැයුණු මෙම ගීතය එස්.ජානකීගේ වෘත්තීය ජීවිතය ආරම්භ කළේය. සිත්ගන්නා කරුණ නම්, ලීලා එය මුලින් ගායනා කිරීමට සුබයියා නායිදුට අවශ්‍ය වීමයි.

කෙසේ වෙතත් ලීලා ගීතයට සම්පූර්ණ සාධාරණයක් ඉටු කළ නොහැකි බව පවසමින් එය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළ අතර එය ගායනා කිරීමට වඩාත් සුදුසු ලෙස සාපේක්ෂ නොදන්නා ජානකී නිර්දේශ කළාය. අවංකකමේ සහ උදාරත්වයේ දුර්ලභ ක්‍රියාවක්!. මීට වසර කිහිපයකට පෙර ජානකී ටොරොන්ටෝ හි ප්‍රසංගයක් පැවැත්වූ විට, ඇය මෙම සිදුවීම සිහිපත් කර “ලීලාම්මාට” කෘතඥතාව පළ කළාය.

ලීලා ඇගේම ලිංගිකත්වයේ ගායක ගායිකාවන් සමඟ යුගල කිහිපයක් ද ගායනා කර ඇත. ගීතිකා සංයෝජන බොහෝ විට සාර්ථක වී ඇත. මනමාගල් චිත්‍රපටයේ MLV සමඟින් ගායනා කළ “ඉන්බමයම්” යුගලය ලීලාගේ නමට හේතු විය. "Vetkamaga irukkuthadi" - "Paar Magakey Paar" හි සූලමංගලම් රාජලක්ෂ්මී සහ "Arivaali" හි "Valiya Needooli" - (Radha) Jayalakshmi වැනි වෙනත් යුගල ගීත ද ඇත. දෙකම භරතනාට්‍ය අංක විය. කෙසේ වෙතත් ලීලා ජික්කි සහ පී. සුශීලා සමඟ පුදුම සහගත ලෙස ක්ලික් කළාය.

ජික්කි සමඟ කිරුළ හිමි වූයේ ජෙමිනි ස්ටූඩියෝස් විසින් "වංචිකෝට්ටෙයි වාලිබන්" චිත්‍රපටයේ ය. සංගීතය "අනර්කලී" කීර්තිමත් C. රාමචන්ද්‍රගෙනි. ජෙමිනී ගනේෂන් වීරයාගේ සෙනෙහස සඳහා තරග වැදුණු වීරවරියන් වූයේ පද්මිණී සහ වෛජයන්තිමාලා යන මහා නටන්න නිළියන් දෙදෙනෙකි. වීරයා සහ දුෂ්ටයා වන වීරප්පා ඉදිරියේ දෙදෙනාම අතර මෙම විශිෂ්ට නර්තන තරඟය පවතී. "කන්නුම් කන්නම් කලන්තු" නම් සම්භාව්‍ය අංකයේ ජික්කි වෛජයන්ති වෙනුවෙන් ද ලීලා පද්මිණී වෙනුවෙන් ද ගායනා කරයි. කොතමංගලම් සුබ්බු ලියූ ගීතය වාචික ද්වන්ධ සටනකි. වචන, තනුව, නැටුම් සහ ගායනය විශිෂ්ට සංයෝජනයක් බව ඔප්පු වේ. එක් අවස්ථාවක වීරප්පා “සබාෂ්! සැරියනා පොට්ටි” (වාව්! නියම තරඟය). එය තිරයේ නැටුම් සඳහා පමණක් නොව, ජිකී සහ ලීලාගේ ඕෆ්-ස්ක්‍රීන් ගායනය සඳහා ද අදාළ විය.

සුශීලා සමඟ ලීලාගේ බොහෝ පැසසුම් ලත් යුගලය වූයේ එන්ටී රාම රාඕ සහ අංජලී දේවි රඟපෑ “ලවකුෂා” ය. “ජෙගම් පුගළුම් පුණ්ණිය කථායි රමනින් කස්තායි, අතයි සේවි කුලිර පඩිදුවොම් කැළුංගල් ඉතයියේ” යන මැරතන් යුගලය මිනිත්තු 12 කින් සමන්විත විය. මරුතකාසිගේ වදන් එම ගීතයේ රාමායණ කතාව කැටිකර ඇත. සංගීතය ඝණ්ඨසාලාගෙනි. ලීලා සහ සුශීලා එය තෙළිඟු භාෂාවෙන් ද චිත්‍රපටයේ තෙළිඟු අනුවාදයේ ගායනා කරති. ශ්‍රී සත්‍ය සායි බාබා එම ගීතයට බෙහෙවින් ඇලුම් කළේය. ඔහු ලීලාව සහ සුශීලාව ලාවා සහ කුෂා ලෙස හැඳින්වූයේ එය තිරයේ ගායනා කළේ ලාවා සහ කුස නිසා බැවිනි.
දෙමළ සිනමාව විසින් ලීලාගේ ගණයේ ගායිකාවක් කෙමෙන් කෙමෙන් අමතක කර දැමීම ඇත්තෙන්ම කණගාටුදායක කරුණක් විය. ලීලා ගීත සඳහා ප්‍රචාරණ කටයුතුවල නිරත වූ කෙනෙක් නොවූ අතර මනරම් ලෙස වියැකී යාමට ඉඩ හැරියේය.

ලීලා දමිළ චිත්‍රපටයකට ගායනා කළේ වසර ගණනාවකට පසුව 1970 වසරේදීය. චිත්‍රපටය වූයේ ‘එංගිරුන්දෝ වන්තල්’ වන අතර එහිදී සකුන්තලෙයි පිළිබඳ සංදර්ශනයක් තිරයේ රඟ දැක්වීය. සීර්ගලී ගෝවින්දරාජන් සහ ලීලාගේ ගීතය ආරම්භ වන්නේ ‘කලිතස මහා කවි කවිය, කන්නි සකුන්තලයි එන්ද්‍රොරු ඕවියම්’ ලෙසිනි. සකුන්තලෙයි වෙනුවෙන් ලීලා හඬ නැගීම යෝග්‍ය විය. කාලිදාස්ගේ වීර කාව්‍යයේ දුෂ්‍යන්තන් සකුන්තලයි අමතක කළා සේම දෙමළ සිනමා ලෝකයට ද ලීලාව අමතක වී තිබුණි.
1970 වසරේ ‘එංගිරුන්තෝ වන්දාල්’ ට පසු 1984 දී ඉලයියරාජා විසින් ඇයව ‘කට්පූර මුල්ලෙයි’ චිත්‍රපටයේ ගායනා කරවන තුරු දිගු නිහැඬියාවකි. නිළිය එම්.එල්. වසන්තකුමාරි සමඟ ලීලා චිත්‍රපට රැසකට එක්ව ගායනා කර ඇත.

දෙමළ සිනමාව හැර තමිල්නාඩුවේ ඕතඩොක්ස් සංගීත සංගම් පවා ලීලාව නොසලකා හැරියේය. ඇය විධිමත් ලෙස පුහුණුව ලත් කර්නාටක ගායිකාවක් වූ නමුත් චෙන්නායි හි ‘සභාස්’ ඇයට බොහෝ පැවරුම් ලබා නොදුන් අතර, ඇයව දෙවන පන්තියේ පසුබිම් ගායිකාවක් ලෙස සැලකුවාය. කේරල සහ ආන්ද්‍රා ඇයට බොහෝ වේදිකා වැඩසටහන් ලබා දුන්නා. කෙසේ වෙතත් මලයාලම් ගුවන් විදුලියෙන් සහ රූපවාහිනියෙන් ඇයට අඛණ්ඩ ප්‍රමුඛත්වයක් ලබා දෙන ලදී.

නීතීඥයෙකු සමඟ ලීලාගේ විවාහය අසාර්ථක විය. ඇය 2005 දී වයස අවුරුදු 71 දී මිය යන තුරුම ඇගේ සොහොයුරිය සහ පවුලේ අය සමඟ චෙන්නායි තදාසන්න ප්‍රදේශයක ජීවත් වූවාය. එවකට තමිල්නාඩු ප්‍රධාන අමාත්‍යවරිය වූ හිටපු සිනමා තරුවක් වූ ජයලලිතා ජයරාම් සිය ශෝක පණිවුඩයේ ලීලාගේ ජීවිතය උචිත ලෙස සාරාංශ කර ඇත. ජයලලිතා ලීලාව විස්තර කළේ "චිත්‍රපට කර්මාන්තයට මෙන්ම කර්නාටක සංගීතයට මලයාලම්, දෙමළ සහ තෙළිඟු යන භාෂා දෙකෙන්ම සිය සියුම් හඬින් සියුම් ගීත ගායනා කරමින් තමාට නමක් දිනාගත් ඉන්දියාවේ ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨතම ගායන ශිල්පිනියක ලෙසය.

දෙමළ ටින්සල් ලෝකය සහ තුප්පහි සභා ලීලාව ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කරන්නට ඇත, නමුත් ඇගේ දහස් ගණන් රසිකයින් කිසිවිටෙක ඇයව ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේ නැත. ඇය ඇතුළත් වැඩසටහනක් තමිල්නාඩුවේ හෝ විදේශයන්හි වේදිකාගත වූ සෑම අවස්ථාවකම බොහෝ දෙනෙක් ඇය බැලීමට හා ඇසීමට රොක් වූහ. අවාසනාවකට, එවැනි අවස්ථා අඩු විය. තාක්‍ෂණය කෙසේ වෙතත් ඇයගේ රසිකයින්ට හුදු ක්ලික් කිරීමකින් මධුර කටහඬට නැවත නැවතත් සවන් දීමට හැකියාව ලැබේ.
දෙමළ සිනමාවට ලීලාව අමතක වන්නට ඇත, නමුත් ඇගේ ගීත අමතක නොවන ලෙස පවතී. පී.ලීලා යන නාමය දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීත ක්ෂේත්‍රයෙන් කිසිදා මැකිය නොහැක.
"Spotlight" තීරුව සඳහා ලියන ලද මෙම ලිපිය 2015 මැයි 23 "Daily FT" හි දිස්වේ. එය මෙතැනින් ප්‍රවේශ විය හැක:
දමිළ සිනමාවේ අමතක නොවන ගීත ගායනා කළ ‘අමතක වූ’ ගායිකාව පී.ලීලා

ta.title: ♬ தமிழ் சினிமாவில் மறக்க முடியாத பாடல்களை பாடிய ‘மறந்த’ பாடகி பி.லீலா ♥
body_lines: (கடந்த நூற்றாண்டின் ஐம்பது அறுபதுகளில் தமிழ்த் திரையுலகில் பாட்டு மகுடம் சூடாத ராணி பி. லீலா. 1934-ல் பிறந்த லீலா 2005-ல் மறைந்தார். 2015-ல் எழுதப்பட்ட இந்தக் கட்டுரை எந்த மாற்றமும் இன்றி இங்கு மீண்டும் பதிவிடப்படுகிறது. மே 19 அன்று லீலாவின் பிறந்த நாளைக் குறிக்கிறது)

P. லீலா என்று பிரபலமாக அறியப்படும் பாடகி பொறையாது லீலாவைப் பற்றி நான் முன்பு எழுதிய கட்டுரையை ஒரு தென்னிந்தியப் பத்திரிக்கை மறுபரிசீலனை செய்யச் சொன்னபோது, ​​இந்த வாரம் நான் இன்ப அதிர்ச்சி அடைந்தேன். 10 ஆண்டுகளுக்கு முன்பு அக்டோபர் 31, 2005 அன்று லீலா காலமானதால், லீலாவின் பிறந்தநாளைக் குறிக்கும் கட்டுரைக்கான கோரிக்கை மிகவும் எதிர்பாராதது. தமிழ் சினிமாவில் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களின் பின்னணிப் பாடகியாக இருந்த அவரது ஒருமுறை வெற்றிகரமான வாழ்க்கை அவரது மறைவுக்கு பல தசாப்தங்களுக்கு முன்பே கிட்டத்தட்ட முடிவுக்கு வந்தது. லீலா, 19 மே 1934 இல் பிறந்தார், ஒரு மலையாளி, அவர் தமிழ்நாட்டின் தலைநகரான சென்னையில் வசித்து வந்தார்.

இன்றைய தலைமுறை திரைப்படப் பாடல் ரசிகர்கள் பி.லீலாவைக் கேட்டிருக்க மாட்டார்கள், கேள்விப்பட்டிருக்க மாட்டார்கள். கடந்த நூற்றாண்டின் ஐம்பதுகள் மற்றும் அறுபதுகளில் தமிழ்த் திரையுலகில் பாடல்களின் முடிசூடா ராணியாக இருந்தார். பி.லீலா தமிழ், மலையாளம், தெலுங்கு, கன்னடம் போன்ற தென்னிந்திய மொழிகளிலும், பெங்காலி மற்றும் சிங்களத்திலும் கூட சுமார் 5,000 திரைப்படப் பாடல்களைப் பாடியுள்ளார். அவரது தமிழ் மொழிபெயர்ப்புகள் சுமார் 1,000 இருக்கலாம். அந்த நாட்களில் அவள் வீட்டுப் பெயர்.

சிங்களத் திரைப்படங்களில் இந்தியக் கலைஞர்களும் தொழில்நுட்பக் கலைஞர்களும் பெரும் பங்கு வகித்த காலம் ஒன்று இருந்தது. சிங்களத் திரையுலகம் அதன் ஆரம்ப கட்டத்தில் இருந்தது மற்றும் இந்திய உள்ளீட்டை பெருமளவு நம்பியிருக்க வேண்டிய கட்டாயம் ஏற்பட்டது. பல சிங்களப் படங்கள் இந்தியாவில் தயாரிக்கப்பட்டன. சிங்களத் திரைப்படங்களின் திரைப்படப் பாடல்கள் இந்தி மற்றும் தமிழ்த் திரைப்படங்களால் பெரிதும் பாதிக்கப்பட்டன. இந்திய இசை இயக்குநர்களான வி.தட்சிணாமூர்த்தி, எஸ்.எம். சுப்பையா நாயுடுவும் வேதாவும் சிங்களப் படங்களுக்கு இசையமைத்துள்ளனர். ஜமுனா ராணி, கே. ராணி, எஸ்.ஜானகி, ஏ.எம்.ராஜா, லீலா போன்ற இந்தியப் பின்னணிப் பாடகர்கள் அக்காலத்தில் சிங்களப் படங்களுக்குப் பாடினார்கள்.

என்னுடைய குழந்தைப் பருவமும், இளமைப் பருவமும் தமிழ்த் திரைப்படப் பாடகர்களான டி.எம். சௌந்தரராஜன், பி.சுசீலா, பி.பி. ஸ்ரீனிவாஸ், சீர்காலி கோவிந்தராஜன், எஸ்.ஜானகி மற்றும் எல்.ஆர். ஈஸ்வரி, உள்ளிட்டோர் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்தினர். லீலா போன்ற பாடகிகள் அப்போது தமிழ் படங்களில் தீவிரமாகப் பாடவில்லை. இருந்தபோதிலும், லீலாதான் சிறந்த பாடகி என்பது சிறுவயதில் என் மனதில் பதிந்திருந்தது. இது ஒரு வெளிப்படையான காரணத்தால் ஏற்பட்டது.

அறுபதுகளின் முற்பகுதியில், “லீலா ஸ்டோர்ஸ்” மற்றும் “லீலா பிரஸ்” உரிமையாளர் திரு.சின்னத்துரை, இலங்கையில் அப்போது அறியப்பட்ட ஒரு விளக்கப்பட நாட்காட்டியை வெளியிட்டார். ஒரு விளக்கப்பட காலண்டர் அல்லது ‘சித்திரக்கலேண்டர்’ முதலில் அறிமுகப்படுத்தப்பட்டபோது ஒரு புதுமையாக இருந்தது. புதிய தயாரிப்பை சந்தைப்படுத்த வானொலி விளம்பரங்கள் பெரிதும் பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டன. பாடகர் மற்றும் காலெண்டருக்கு இடையே உள்ள "லீலா" பெயரின் ஒற்றுமை தயாரிப்பை விளம்பரப்படுத்த புத்திசாலித்தனமாக பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டது.

பெரும்பாலான விளம்பரங்களில் முதலில் லீலாவின் பிரபலமான பாடலான 'எங்குமே ஆனந்தம்' அல்லது 'கொஞ்சும் சலங்கை ஒலி கெத்து' போன்ற ஒரு வரி இருக்கும். லீலாவின் பாடல்களைப் போலவே, லீலா காலண்டரும் சிறந்தது அல்லது லீலாவின் பாடலைப் போலவே, பெயரிடப்பட்ட நாட்காட்டியும் மகிழ்ச்சியைத் தந்தது என்று ஒரு குரல் கூறுகிறது. அந்த வயதில் லீலா சிறந்த பாடகி என்பதை இது எனக்குள் அழியாத தாக்கத்தை ஏற்படுத்தியது. நிச்சயமாக பிற்காலங்களில் ஒருவர் அதிகமான பழைய பாடல்களைக் கேட்டு, முந்தைய அந்த நைட்டிங்கேல்கள் அனைத்தும் அவற்றின் சொந்த வழியில் தனித்துவமானது என்பதை உணர்ந்தார்.

என் அம்மாவும் லீலாவின் தீவிர ரசிகை. அவரது விருப்பமான தமிழ் திரைப்பட பாடகர் இரட்டையர்கள் அல்லது ‘ஜோடி’ லீலா மற்றும் புகழ்பெற்ற பாடகி கன்டாசாலா. லீலா ஒரு மலையாளி மற்றும் கன்டாசாலா ஒரு தெலுங்கர். ஆனாலும் அவர்கள் தமிழ் மற்றும் தெலுங்கு படங்களில் பாடி பிரபலமான ஜோடி. குரல் வாரியாக, கன்டாசாலா ஒரு பாஸ் மற்றும் லீலா ஒரு சோப்ரானோ ஆனால் இருவரும் நன்றாக ஒத்திசைந்தனர். கன்டசாலா மற்றும் லீலாவின் குரல்கள் ஒருவருக்கொருவர் உருவாக்கப்பட்டவை என்று என் அம்மா அடிக்கடி கூறுவார். 'பாதாள பைரவி,' 'மாயா பஜார்' மற்றும் 'பிரேம பாசம்' போன்ற படங்களில், 'அமைதி இல்லதேன் மனமே,' 'என்னதான் உன் பிரேமையோ,' 'காதலே தெய்வீக', 'நீதானா என்னை,' போன்ற எவர்கிரீன் எண்களுடன் இந்த ஜோடி கச்சிதமாக வெற்றி பெற்றது. 'ஆஹா இன்ப நிலவினிலே' மற்றும் 'வீசும் தென்றலிலே' போன்றவை.

கன்டசாலாவைத் தவிர, ஏ.எம். போன்ற ஆண் பாடகர்களுடன் லீலாவின் குரல் நன்றாகக் கலந்திருந்தது. ராஜா, டி.எம். சௌந்தரராஜன் மற்றும் சீர்காலி கோவிந்தராஜன் ஆகியோரும். ஏ.எம்.ராஜாவுடன் அவர் பாடிய பல டூயட்களில் "மிஸ்சியம்மா"வில் "வாராயோ வெண்ணிலாவே", "அசை மகன்" இல் "ஓடமீதிருந்தே" மற்றும் "குணசுந்தரி"யில் "நான் செய்த பூஜாபலம்" ஆகியவை அடங்கும். சௌந்தரராஜனுடன் அவரது மறக்கமுடியாத எண்கள் சிலவாகும்: “முகத்தினில் முகம் பார்க்கலாம்” (தங்கபாடுமை), “நெஞ்சில் குடியிருக்கும்” (இரும்புத்திரை), “தென்றல் உறங்கிடக்கூடுமதி” (சங்கிலி தேவன்), “தலையம் பூமுடிச்சு” (பாகாபிரி சூழ்ந்தோக்கி) , “இகலோகமே” (தங்கமலை ரகசியம்) “பூவிந்திரி மனமேது” (தங்கப்பத்துமை) மற்றும் “நிலவே நீ இந்த” (பட்டினத்தார்).சீர்கலி மற்றும் லீலாவும் “எல்லையில்லா இன்பத்திலே” (சக்கரவர்த்தி திருமகள்) , “ஆறு. நல்லவன் வல்வன்) மற்றும் "கலத்தை மாற்றினன்" (மனிதன் மாறவில்லை).

நான் வளர்ந்தபோது முன்பு கூறியது போல் - தமிழ் திரைப்படங்கள் மற்றும் தமிழ் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களின் டயட்டில் - பின்னணி பாடகர்களான கன்டசாலா, லீலா, ஏ.எம். ராஜா, ஜிக்கி, சிதம்பரம் ஜெயராமன், எம்.எல். வசந்தகுமாரி, திருச்சி லோகநாதன் போன்றோர் தமிழ்ப் படங்களில் சரிவைச் சந்தித்தனர். இது அவர்களின் எந்தக் குறைபாட்டாலும் ஏற்படவில்லை. உண்மையில் அப்போது அவர்கள் உச்சத்தில் இருந்தார்கள். துரதிர்ஷ்டவசமாக அவர்களுக்கு தமிழ் படங்களில் பாட வாய்ப்புகள் கிடைக்கவில்லை.

இது நான்கு காரணங்களால் ஏற்பட்டது. ஒன்று, குறிப்பிட்ட திரைப்பட இசையமைப்பாளர்கள் மற்றும் திரைப்பட இயக்குநர்கள் பல்வேறு காரணங்களுக்காக அவர்களைத் தவிர்க்கத் தொடங்கினர். மற்றொன்று என்னவென்றால், பலவிதமான பாடகர்கள் பின்னணி இசையமைக்கும் பழைய வழக்கம் ஒரு படத்திற்கு இரண்டு அல்லது மூன்று பாடகர்களை மட்டுமே வைத்திருக்கும் நடைமுறைக்கு வழிவகுத்தது. மூன்றாவதாக ஒரு படத்தின் பாடல்களின் எண்ணிக்கையும் படிப்படியாகக் குறைக்கப்பட்டது. கடைசியாக அன்றைய முன்னணி நடிகர், நடிகைகள் டி.எம். சௌந்தரராஜனும், பி.சுசீலாவும் அவர்களுக்காகப் பாட வேண்டும்.

இதனால் லீலா போன்ற பாடகிகளுக்கு அறுபதுகளின் தொடக்கத்திற்குப் பிறகு தமிழில் ஒரு சில வாய்ப்புகள் மட்டுமே கிடைத்தன. அதனால் அந்த வருடங்களில் நான் பார்த்த புதிய தமிழ் படங்களில் இவரது பாடல்கள் இல்லை. ஆனால் எப்போதும் ‘ரேடியோ சிலோன்’ இருந்தது. நானும் தீவிர சினிமா ரசிகனாகி பழைய தமிழ்ப் படங்களைத் தேடினேன். அவற்றில் பலவற்றில் லீலாவின் பாடல்கள் இடம்பெற்றன.

லீலாவின் குரலும் பாடும் பாணியும் என்னை மிகவும் கவர்ந்தன. இது அவரது சமகாலத்தவர்களில் குறைந்தது ஐந்து பேரின் ஒப்பற்ற ஆனால் இனிமையான கலவையாகும் - எம்.எல். வசந்தகுமாரி, ஜிக்கி, (ராதா) ஜெயலட்சுமி, டி.எஸ். பகவதி மற்றும் ஏ.பி.கோமளா. அது ஒரு உண்மையான "பஞ்சாமிர்தம்" (கடவுளுக்கு "பிரசாதமாக" வழங்கப்படும் பால், தேன், தயிர், நெய் மற்றும் பழச்சாறு ஆகியவற்றின் அமுத கலவை போல இருந்தது. தனிப்பாடலாகவோ, டூயட் பாடலாகவோ அல்லது கோரஸ் பாடலின் ஒரு பகுதியாகவோ (கோஷ்டி கானம்) லீலாவின் எதிரொலிக்கும் குரல் எப்போதும் சத்தமாகவும் தெளிவாகவும் ஒலிக்கும்.

1934 ஆம் ஆண்டு மே 19 ஆம் தேதி கேரளாவின் பால்காட் அருகே உள்ள சித்தூரில் பொறயத்து லீலா பிறந்தார். அவரது ஆரம்ப காலம் கொச்சியில் இருந்தது, அங்கு அவரது தந்தை குஞ்சன் மேனன் பள்ளி மாஸ்டராக இருந்தார். லீலாவின் தாயார் மீனாட்சி குட்டியை திருமணத்திற்குப் பிறகு கர்நாடக இசை கற்க ஊக்குவித்தார். தந்தை தனது மகளின் இசைத் திறனையும் குரல் வரம்பையும் மிகச் சிறிய வயதிலேயே அடையாளம் கண்டுகொண்டார். அவர் இசைப் பாடங்களுக்கு ஏற்பாடு செய்தார், மேலும் சிறுவயதில் லீலாவை தினமும் அதிகாலை 4-6 மணிக்குள் தனியாக பயிற்சி செய்ய வைத்தார்.

ஆங்கிலேயர் ஆட்சியின் போது சென்னை அல்லது மெட்ராஸ் தென்னிந்திய தலைநகராக இருந்தது. இது திராவிட மொழி பாரம்பரியம் கொண்டவர்களுக்கு கலாச்சார மெக்காவாக இருந்தது. தனது மகளின் திறனை உணர்ந்து, இடம் மாற்றம் தேவை என்பதை உணர்ந்த குஞ்சன் மேனன், 1944 இல் தனது வேலையை ராஜினாமா செய்துவிட்டு, மீண்டும் சென்னைக்கு வந்தார். லீலா பத்தமடை கிருஷ்ண ஐயர் மற்றும் மருதுவாக்குடி ராஜகோபால் ஐயர் ஆகியோருடன் சென்னை ராம பாகவதரின் கீழ் தனது இசைப் பயிற்சியைத் தொடர்ந்தார். தந்தையும் மகளும் அனைத்து கர்னாடக பாடல் நிகழ்வுகளுக்கும் புகழ்பெற்ற பேச்சாளர்களால் வந்திருந்தனர்.

1944-ல் மெட்ராஸ் விஸ்வத் சபா நடத்திய ‘ராகம்-தாளம்-பல்லவி’ போட்டியில் முதலாவதாக வந்து முத்திரை பதித்தார் லீலா. நடுவர்களாக ஜி.என். பாலசுப்ரமணியம் மற்றும் பால்காட் மணி ஐயர். ஆந்திர மகிளா சமிதி வழக்கமான நிகழ்ச்சிகளுக்கு ஏற்பாடு செய்தபோது இது அவருக்கு ஓய்வு கொடுத்தது. அவர் ஒரு வளர்ந்து வரும் கர்நாடக இசைக்கலைஞராக ஒரு பெயரை உருவாக்கத் தொடங்கினார். அப்பாவோ மகளோ அப்போது படங்களில் பாட விரும்பவில்லை.

நாற்பதுகளில் இந்திய சினிமாவில் பின்னணிப் பாடல் என்று அழைக்கப்படும் ஒரு புதிய நிகழ்வைக் கண்டது. நடிகர்கள் தங்கள் பாடல்களைப் பாடுவதற்குப் பதிலாக, அவர்கள் திரையில் வார்த்தைகளை உதடுகளால் ஒத்திசைக்கிறார்கள். வழக்கமான பாடகர்கள் பாடல்களைப் பாடிக்கொண்டிருந்தனர். மறுபதிவு மூலம் இரண்டையும் இணைத்து இறுதி ஒலிப்பதிவு செய்யப்பட்டது. பல தொழில்முறை பாடகர்கள் படங்களில் பாடி பணத்தையும் புகழையும் சம்பாதித்து வந்தனர்.

இசையமைப்பாளர் பத்மநாப சாஸ்திரி லீலா பாடியதைக் கேட்டு மனம் கவர்ந்தார். ஒரு சலுகை வழங்கப்பட்டது. அப்பாவும் மகளும் முதலில் தயங்கினார்கள். புதிய ஊடகம் குறித்து அவர்களுக்கு சந்தேகம் இருந்தது. இறுதியாக அவர்கள் ஒப்புக்கொண்டனர். லீலா 1948-ல் சினிமாவில் பின்னணிப் பாடலுக்குள் நுழைந்தார். அது கே.ஆர் நடித்த ‘கண்கணம்’ தமிழ்த் திரைப்படம். ராமசாமி மற்றும் மேனகா. லீலா தரையில் அமர்ந்து மைக்கில் பாடினாள். அவரது நான்கு பாடல்கள் படத்திற்காக பதிவு செய்யப்பட்டன. அதன் பிறகு அவள் திரும்பிப் பார்க்கவே இல்லை.

1949 அவரது வாழ்க்கையில் திருப்புமுனையாக அமைந்தது. அவர் திரைப்படங்களுக்கு இசையமைத்து வந்த சி.ஆர்.சுப்பராமன் (CRS) என்ற இசைக் கலைஞரின் கீழ் இசைப் பாடங்களைத் தொடங்கினார். CRS தனது மாணவருக்கு வாய்ப்புகளை வழங்கியது. 1949 ஆம் ஆண்டு ‘பக்த கபீர்’ என்ற கன்னடப் படத்தில் சிஆர்எஸ்-க்காக லீலா முதன்முதலாகப் பாடினார். சிஆர்எஸ் தமிழில் ‘மோகினி’ மற்றும் ‘வேலைக்காரி’ ஆகிய படங்களிலும் லீலாவுக்கு வாய்ப்புகளை வழங்கினார். கே.வி.யுடன் ‘மோகினி’க்காகப் பாடிய ‘ஆஹா இவர் யாரடி’. ஜானகியும், ‘வேலைக்காரி’க்காக ‘ஒரிதந்தனிலே’ படமும் ஹிட். 1950களில் லீலா அனைத்து தென்னிந்தியப் படங்களிலும் பாடத் தொடங்கினார். ஒரு பாடும் நட்சத்திரம் பிறந்தது.

லீலாவை ஒரு உன்னதமான எண் மூலம் ஆர்வலர்கள் மற்றும் சாமானியர்களை ஒரே மாதிரியாகக் கவர உதவியதும் சுப்பராமன் தான். அது ‘மணமகள்’ படத்துக்காகவும், ‘திருவாங்கூர் சகோதரிகளான’ லலிதா மற்றும் பத்மினியின் நடனத்துக்கான டூயட் பாடலும். பழம்பெரும் பாடகர் எம்.எல் உடன் லீலா பாட வேண்டும் என்று சிஆர்எஸ் விரும்பினார். வசந்தகுமாரி. லீலா சேர்ந்து பாடுவதற்கு பதட்டமாக இருந்தாள், ஆனால் அவளுடைய வழிகாட்டியால் வற்புறுத்தப்பட்டாள். அது ராகமாலிகா (மெட்லி) ‘எல்லாம் இன்ப மாயம்’.

திரையில் லலிதாவும் பத்மினியும் தாள இயக்கத்தில் நடனமாடியபோதும் எம்.எல்.வி.யும் லீலாவும் ராகத்திலிருந்து ராகத்திற்கு வெளியே சிரமமின்றி நகர்ந்தனர். லீலா எம்.எல்.வி.யுடன் தனக்கென ஒரு தனித்துவத்தை வைத்திருந்தார் மற்றும் கிளாசிக்கல் மெல்லிசையின் சிறந்த பாடகியாக தனது பெயரை நிறுவினார். தற்செயலாக எம்.எல்.வி மற்றும் லீலா ஆகிய இருவர் மட்டுமே கர்நாடக இசையில் முறையான பயிற்சி பெற்ற அந்த தலைமுறையின் பின்னணிப் பாடகர்கள்.

லீலா எந்த ஒரு பாடலையும் எந்த வேகத்திலும் பாடக்கூடிய ஒரு பாடகி. கிளாசிக்கல், செமி கிளாசிக்கல், லைட் மற்றும் ஃபோக் பாடல்கள் அனைத்தும் அவளது பலம். பக்தி, நடனம், ஜிப்சி, நாட்டுப்புற, காதல் பாடல்கள் அனைத்தும் அவரது திறமையின் ஒரு பகுதியாக இருந்தன. அவள் மெல்லிசையை எளிதாகப் புரிந்துகொண்டு எல்லா ஒலிகளுடனும் பாடுவாள். அவள் ‘ஸ்ருதியை’ குறைத்து, காமமாகப் பாடினாள், அவளுடைய மெல்லிய குரலுக்கு முழு வீச்சு கொடுத்தாள்.

லீலா எந்தவிதமான சலசலப்பும், கோபமும் இல்லாமல் எளிமையான மற்றும் அடக்கமான நபராக இருந்தாள். அவர் தனது இசை இயக்குனர்கள் மற்றும் சக பாடகர்கள் அனைவருக்கும் நன்றாக ஒத்துழைத்தார். பத்மினி, சாவித்திரி, ஜமுனா, அஞ்சலி தேவி, டி.ஆர் என பல நடிகைகளுக்காக லீலா பாடியுள்ளார். ராஜகுமாரி, ராஜசுலோசனா, விஜயகுமாரி மற்றும் சரோஜாதேவி கூட. அவரது குரல் பத்மினி மற்றும் சாவித்திரிக்கு மிகவும் பொருத்தமாக இருந்தது. சி.ஆர்.சுப்பராமன், கன்டசாலா, கே.வி போன்ற இசையமைப்பாளர்களுக்காகவும் பாடியுள்ளார். மகாதேவன், எஸ்.எம். சுப்பையா நாயுடு, ராஜேஸ்வர ராவ், சி.ராமச்சந்திரா, ஜி.ராமநாதன் மற்றும் விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி. ஏறக்குறைய ஒவ்வொரு இசையமைப்பாளரும் அவரிடமிருந்து சிறந்ததை வெளிப்படுத்தினர், இருப்பினும் CRS, கன்டசாலா, KVM மற்றும் ஜி. ராமநாதன் அவரது திறமையைப் பயன்படுத்துவதில் சிறந்து விளங்கினர்.
லீலாவின் தமிழ்ப் படங்களில் எண்ணற்ற தனிப்பாடல்கள் உள்ளன. என் கருத்துப்படி, அவரது தனிப்பாடல்களில் மிகச்சிறந்த நான்கு ‘மிஸ்சியம்மா,’ ‘வஞ்சிக்கோட்டை வாலிபன்’ மற்றும் ‘தங்கப்பத்துமை’.

‘மிஸ்சியம்மா’ படத்தில் ஜெமினி கணேஷ் மற்றும் நிஜ வாழ்க்கை மனைவி சாவித்திரி நடித்துள்ளனர். இரண்டு குறிப்பிடத்தக்க லீலா தனிப்பாடல்கள் ‘மாயமே நான் அறிவேன்’ மற்றும் ‘எனையாலும் மேரி மாதா’. பிந்தையது கன்னி மேரிக்கு ஒரு வேண்டுகோள் லீலா ஒரு பக்தியுள்ள இந்துவாக இருந்தாலும் உணர்ச்சிவசப்பட்ட உச்சத்தில் பாடப்பட்டது. இது ‘ஞானசௌந்தரி’ – ‘அருள் தரும் தெய்வ மாதவே’ படத்தில் வரும் மேரியின் ஜிக்கி-பெரியநாயகி எண்ணுக்கு இணையாக உள்ளது.

‘வஞ்சிக்கோட்டை வாலிபன்’ படத்தின் தனிப்பாடல் ‘வெண்ணிலாவே தன்மதியே என்னுதானே வா வா’. இது ஒரு இரவு நேர படகு காட்சியில் பத்மினிக்காக திரையில் அமைக்கப்பட்ட ஒரு இனிமையான மெல்லிசை. மெல்லிசை காற்று, நிலவொளி பிரகாசம், தண்ணீரில் அலைகள் மற்றும் எல்லாவற்றிற்கும் மேலாக படகின் மெதுவான, தாள இயக்கம் ஆகியவற்றை மெல்லிசை தூண்டுகிறது. இது ஒருவரை ஒரு அமானுஷ்ய வளிமண்டலத்திற்கு கொண்டு செல்கிறது.

நான்காவது தனிப்பாடல் ‘தங்கப்பத்துமை’யில் ‘சொல்லம்மா வாய் திறந்து’ என்ற சோக எண். அதை பத்மினி திரையில் நடிக்கிறார். இசை விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி. லீலா இதுவரை தமிழ்ப் படங்களுக்காகப் பாடிய பாடலிலேயே மிகவும் கடினமான பாடல் இது என்று பதிவு செய்துள்ளார். காலை 9 மணி முதல் இரவு 9 மணி வரை ஓட்டுப்பதிவு நடந்தது. 12 மணி நேர இடைவெளியில். அது பாத்தோஸ் நிறைந்ததாக இருந்ததால், லீலா உணர்ச்சிவசப்பட்டாள். விஸ்வநாதன் எளிதில் திருப்தி அடையவில்லை, லீலாவை இடைவிடாமல் உழைக்கச் செய்தார். அதன் முடிவில் லீலா கதறி அழுதாள். லீலாவின் பாடலும் பத்மினியின் நடிப்பும் பாடல் காட்சியை பெரும் வெற்றியடையச் செய்தது.

மேற்கூறிய நான்கையும் லீலாவின் நான்கு சிறந்த தனிப்பாடல்களாகக் கருதினாலும், அவருடைய தனிப்பாடல்களில் எனக்கு மிகவும் பிடித்தது ‘டாக்டர். சாவித்திரி’. இசையமைப்பாளர் ஜி. ராமநாதன் இசையமைத்துள்ளார், அவர் வழக்கமாக அவர் பணிபுரிந்த திரைப்படங்களில் லீலாவுக்கு அதிக வாய்ப்பைக் கொடுத்தார். பாடல் கிழக்கு மற்றும் மேற்கத்திய பாரம்பரிய இசையை அடிப்படையாகக் கொண்டது. இப்பாடல் நடிகை அஞ்சலி தேவி மற்றும் காட்சியில் பியானோ வாசிக்கும் "வீணை" மேஸ்ட்ரோ எஸ். பாலச்சந்தர் மீது படமாக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது.

லீலா சோப்ரானிக் டோன்களில் பியானோ இசையுடன் பாடுகிறார். பியானோ இடைச்செருகல்கள் கிளாசிக்கல் இயக்கத்தின் சிறிய மாறுபாடுகள். லீலா நம்பமுடியாத இனிமையின் 'ஆஹார பிருகா'வுடன் தொடங்குகிறது மற்றும் இதேபோன்ற இடைவெளிகளுடன் தொடர்கிறது. வார்த்தைகளும் இசை மற்றும் பாடலின் சிறப்பைப் போற்றுகின்றன. அவள் பாடும் ‘ஆனந்த கீதம்’ (மகிழ்ச்சியின் பாடல்) ஒரு “அமுத சாகரம்” (அம்ப்ரோசியா கடல்). எவ்வளவு உண்மை! ஏறக்குறைய லீலா கையெழுத்திட்ட மிஷன் ஸ்டேட்மென்ட் போல இந்தப் பாடல் இருக்கிறது.

"உத்தம புத்திரன்" படத்தில் "கத்திருப்பன் கமலக்கண்ணன்" என்ற பிரபலமான மற்றும் பாராட்டப்பட்ட தனிப்பாடல்களில், பத்மினி மற்றும் அவரது இளைய சகோதரி ராகினியின் பாரதம் காட்சிக்காக ராமநாதன் அமைத்த ராகமாலிகாவை லீலா பாடுகிறார். லீலா ஒரு ராகத்திலிருந்து இன்னொரு ராகத்திற்கு சீராக நகர்கிறது. பாடகரை எம்.எல்.வி என்று பலர் தவறாக நினைக்கும் பாடல் மிகவும் குறிப்பிடத்தக்கது. லீலா பாரம்பரிய ரத்தினங்களில் மற்றொன்று "அன்னையின் ஆனை"யில் சாருகேசி ராகத்தில் அமைந்த "நீயே கதி ஈஸ்வரி". இசையமைப்பாளர் எஸ்.எம்.சுப்பையா நாயுடு, லீலாவின் பணியை வெகுவாகப் பாராட்டினார்.

“கொஞ்சும் சலங்கை” படத்திற்கு இசையமைத்தவர் சுப்பையா. இதற்கு லீலா “கொஞ்சும் சலங்கை ஒலி கேட்டு, நெஞ்சில் பொங்குதம்மா புதிய பாட்டு” பாடலைப் பாடுகிறார். ஆனால் படத்தின் ஹிட் பாடல் "சிங்கார வேலனே தேவா". காருகுறிச்சி அருணாசலத்தின் நாதஸ்வரத்துடன் இணைந்த இந்தப் பாடல்தான் எஸ்.ஜானகியின் வாழ்க்கையைத் துவக்கியது. சுப்பையா நாயுடு முதலில் லீலா பாட வேண்டும் என்று விரும்பினார்.

இருப்பினும் லீலா பாடலுக்கு முழு நியாயம் செய்ய முடியாது என்று மறுத்து, ஒப்பீட்டளவில் அறியப்படாத ஜானகியைப் பாடுவதற்கு மிகவும் பொருத்தமானவர் என்று பரிந்துரைத்தார். வெளிப்படையான மற்றும் பெருந்தன்மையின் அரிய செயல்!. சில வருடங்களுக்கு முன்பு ஜானகி ரொறன்ரோவில் நிகழ்ச்சி நடத்தியபோது, ​​இந்தச் சம்பவத்தை நினைவு கூர்ந்து, “லீலாம்மாவுக்கு” ​​நன்றியைத் தெரிவித்தார்.

லீலா தனது சொந்த பாலின பாடகர்களுடன் பல டூயட்களையும் பாடியுள்ளார். பாடலாசிரியர் சேர்க்கைகள் பெரும்பாலும் வெற்றி பெற்றுள்ளன. "மணமகள்" படத்தில் எம்.எல்.வி.யுடன் பாடிய "இன்பமயம்" டூயட் தான் லீலாவின் பெயரை உருவாக்கியது. "வெட்கமாக இருக்குதடி" - "பார் மகனே பார்" படத்தில் சூலமங்கலம் ராஜலட்சுமி மற்றும் "அறிவாளி"யில் "வாழிய நீடூலி" - (ராதா) ஜெயலட்சுமி போன்ற பிறருடன் மற்ற டூயட்களும் உள்ளன. இரண்டும் பரதநாட்டிய எண்கள். இருப்பினும் லீலா ஜிக்கி மற்றும் பி. சுசீலாவுடன் அற்புதமாக கிளிக் செய்தார்.

ஜெமினி ஸ்டுடியோவின் “வஞ்சிக்கோட்டை வாலிபன்” படத்தில் ஜிக்கியுடன் மகுடம் சூடினார். “அனார்கலி” புகழ் சி.ராமச்சந்திரா இசையமைத்தார். நாயகன் ஜெமினி கணேசனின் பாசத்துக்குப் போட்டியிடும் கதாநாயகிகளாக பத்மினி, வைஜெயந்திமாலா ஆகிய இரு பெரும் நடனக் கலைஞர்கள் இருந்தனர். ஹீரோ மற்றும் வில்லன் வீரப்பா முன்னிலையில் இருவருக்கும் இடையே இந்த அற்புதமான நடனப் போட்டி உள்ளது. “கண்ணும் கண்ணும் கலந்து” என்ற கிளாசிக் பாடலில் வைஜெயந்திக்காக ஜிக்கியும், பத்மினிக்காக லீலாவும் பாடுகிறார்கள். கொத்தமங்கலம் சுப்பு எழுதிய பாடல் வாய் சண்டை. வார்த்தைகள், மெல்லிசை, நடனம் மற்றும் பாடல் ஆகியவை ஒரு சிறந்த கலவையாக நிரூபிக்கின்றன. ஒரு கட்டத்தில் வீரப்பா “சபாஷ்! சரியான போட்டி” (அட! பெரும் போட்டி). இது திரையில் நடனமாடுவதற்கு மட்டுமல்ல, ஜிக்கி மற்றும் லீலாவின் ஆஃப்-ஸ்கிரீன் பாடலுக்கும் பொருந்தும்.

என்.டி.ராமராவ் மற்றும் அஞ்சலி தேவி நடித்த "லவகுஷா"வில் சுசீலாவுடன் லீலா மிகவும் பாராட்டப்பட்ட டூயட். “ஜெகம் புகழும் புண்ணிய கதை ரமணனின் கஷ்டமே, அதைச் செவி குளிர படிடுவோம் கெழுங்கள் இதயே” என்ற மாரத்தான் 12 நிமிட டூயட். மருதகாசியின் வார்த்தைகள் அந்தப் பாடலில் ராமாயணக் கதையை பொதிந்துள்ளன. இசை கன்டாசாலா. படத்தின் தெலுங்கு பதிப்பில் லீலாவும் சுசீலாவும் அதை தெலுங்கிலும் பாடுகிறார்கள். ஸ்ரீ சத்ய சாய் பாபா அந்தப் பாடலைப் பெரிதும் கவர்ந்தார். லீலாவையும் சுசீலாவையும் லாவா மற்றும் குசா என்று அவர் குறிப்பிட்டார், ஏனென்றால் லாவாவும் குசாவும் அதை திரையில் பாடுகிறார்கள்.
லீலாவின் திறமையான ஒரு பாடகி தமிழ் சினிமாவால் மெல்ல மெல்ல மறதிக்கு அனுப்பப்பட்டது உண்மையில் பரிதாபமாக இருந்தது. லீலா பாடல்களுக்கு கேன்வாஸ் செய்வதில் ஒருவரல்ல, மேலும் தன்னை அழகாக மங்க அனுமதித்தார்.

பல வருட இடைவெளிக்குப் பிறகு 1970ல் ஒரு தமிழ்ப் படத்தில் லீலா பாடினார். ‘எங்கிருந்தோ வந்தாள்’ திரைப்படம், அங்கு சகுந்தலையில் ஒரு சிறுகதை திரையில் நிகழ்த்தப்பட்டது. சீர்காலி கோவிந்தராஜன் மற்றும் லீலாவின் பாடல் ‘கலிதாச மகா கவி காவியம், கன்னி சகுந்தலை என்றொரு ஓவியம்’ என்று தொடங்குகிறது. சகுந்தலைக்காக லீலா குரல் கொடுத்தது பொருத்தமாக இருந்தது. சகுந்தலையை மறந்த காளிதாஸின் காவியத்தில் துஷ்யந்தனைப் போலவே தமிழ்த் திரையுலகமும் லீலாவை மறந்துவிட்டது.

1970ல் ‘எங்கிருந்தோ வந்தாள்’ படத்திற்குப் பிறகு, 1984ல் இளையராஜா ‘கட்பூர முல்லை’ படத்தில் பாட வைக்கும் வரை நீண்ட அமைதி நிலவியது. நடிகை எம்.எல். வசந்தகுமாரியுடன் லீலா பல படங்களில் இணைந்து பாடியுள்ளார்.

தமிழ் சினிமாவைத் தவிர தமிழ்நாட்டின் மரபுவழி இசை சங்கங்கள் கூட லீலாவைப் புறக்கணித்தன. அவர் முறையாகப் பயிற்றுவிக்கப்பட்ட கர்நாடகப் பாடகி, ஆனால் சென்னையைச் சேர்ந்த ‘சபாஸ்’ அவருக்குப் பல பணிகளை வழங்கவில்லை, அவரை இரண்டாம் தர பின்னணிப் பாடகியாகக் கருதினார். கேரளாவும் ஆந்திராவும் அவருக்கு பல மேடை நிகழ்ச்சிகளை வழங்கின. இருப்பினும் மலையாள வானொலி மற்றும் தொலைக்காட்சி மூலம் அவருக்கு தொடர்ந்து முக்கியத்துவம் அளிக்கப்பட்டது.

ஒரு வழக்கறிஞருடன் லீலாவின் திருமணம் தோல்வியடைந்தது. அவர் 2005 ஆம் ஆண்டு தனது 71வது வயதில் இறக்கும் வரை தனது சகோதரி மற்றும் குடும்பத்துடன் சென்னை புறநகரில் வசித்து வந்தார். முன்னாள் திரைப்பட நட்சத்திரம் ஜெயலலிதா ஜெயராம், அப்போதைய தமிழக முதல்வராக இருந்தவர், லீலாவின் வாழ்க்கையைத் தனது இரங்கல் செய்தியில் பொருத்தமாகச் சுருக்கமாகக் கூறியுள்ளார். லீலாவை, "இந்தியாவின் மிகச்சிறந்த குரல் இசைக்கலைஞர்களில் ஒருவர், மலையாளம், தமிழ் மற்றும் தெலுங்கு ஆகிய மொழிகளில் திரைப்படத் துறையிலும், கர்நாடக இசையிலும் தனது மெல்லிய குரலில் நேர்த்தியான பாடல்களைப் பாடி தனக்கென ஒரு பெயரைப் பெற்றவர்" என்று ஜெயலலிதா விவரித்தார்.

தமிழ் டின்ஸல் உலகமும், கேவலமான சபாக்களும் லீலாவை நிராகரித்திருக்கலாம், ஆனால் அவரது ஆயிரக்கணக்கான ரசிகர்கள் அவரை ஒருபோதும் நிராகரிக்கவில்லை. தமிழ்நாட்டிலோ அல்லது வெளிநாட்டிலோ அவர் பங்கேற்கும் நிகழ்ச்சிகள் அரங்கேற்றப்பட்டால், அவரைப் பார்க்கவும் கேட்கவும் பலர் குவிந்தனர். துரதிர்ஷ்டவசமாக, அத்தகைய சந்தர்ப்பங்கள் குறைவாகவே இருந்தன. இருப்பினும், தொழில்நுட்பம் அவரது ரசிகர்களை ஒரே கிளிக்கில் இனிய குரலை மீண்டும் மீண்டும் கேட்க உதவுகிறது.
தமிழ் சினிமா லீலாவை மறந்திருக்கலாம், ஆனால் அவரது பாடல்கள் மறக்க முடியாதவை. பி.லீலா என்ற பெயரை தமிழ்த் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களில் இருந்து ஒருபோதும் அழிக்க முடியாது.
“ஸ்பாட்லைட்” நெடுவரிசைக்காக எழுதப்பட்ட இந்தக் கட்டுரை மே 23, 2015 இன் “டெய்லி எஃப்டி”யில் வெளிவந்துள்ளது. அதை இங்கே அணுகலாம்:
தமிழ் சினிமாவில் மறக்க முடியாத பாடல்களை பாடிய ‘மறந்த’ பாடகி பி.லீலா

en.title: ♬ P. Leela, ‘Forgotten’ Singer Who Sang Unforgettable  Songs In Tamil Cinema ♥
body_lines: (P. Leela was the uncrowned queen of song in the realm of Tamil films during the fifties and sixties of the last century.Born in 1934, Leela passed away in 2005.This Article written in 2015 is re-posted here without any changes to denote Leela’s Birthdy on May 19)

I was pleasantly surprised this week when a South Indian journal asked me to revise an article I had written earlier about the singer Porayathu Leela – popularly known as P. Leela. The request for an article to denote Leela’s birthday was most unexpected as she had passed away 10 years ago on October 31st 2005.Her  once successful career as a  Play back singer of film songs in Tamil cinema had virtually ended decades before her demise. Leela, born on 19 May 1934, was ethnically a Malayalee who resided and died in the Tamil Nadu state capital of Chennai.

The present generation of film song fans may have never heard her or even heard of P. Leela. She was the uncrowned queen of song in the realm of Tamil films during the fifties and sixties of the last century. P. Leela has sung about 5,000 film songs in South Indian languages such as Tamil, Malayalam, Telugu, Kannada and even in Bengalee and Sinhala. Her Tamil renditions may number around 1,000. She was a household name in those days.

There was a time when Indian artistes and technicians played a big role in Sinhala films. The Sinhala film industry was in its nascent stages and forced to rely on Indian input to a great extent. Many Sinhala films were produced in India. Film songs in Sinhala films were heavily influenced by Hindi and Tamil films. Indian music directors such as V. Dakshinamoorthy, S.M. Subbiah Nayudu and Vedha composed music for Sinhala films. Indian playback singers like Jamuna Rani, K. Rani, S.Janaki ,AM Rajah and Leela used to sing for Sinhala films in those days.

My years of childhood and early teens was an era where Tamil film singers like T.M. Soundararajan, P. Susheela, P.B. Sreenivas, Seergali Govindarajan, S. Janaki and L.R. Eeswari, etc. dominated. Singers like Leela were not actively singing in Tamil films then. Despite this it was etched in my consciousness as a kid that Leela was the best singer of them all. This was due to a rather facetious reason.

The early sixties saw the “Leela stores” cum “Leela press” owner Mr.Sinnathurai bringing out an illustrated calendar in Ceylon as Sri Lanka was known then. An illustrated calendar or ‘Sithirakkalendar’ was a novelty when first introduced. Radio advertisements were used greatly to market the new product. The similarity of the “Leela” name between the singer and calendar was smartly used to promote the product.

Most ads would first have a line from a popular song by Leela like ‘Engume Aanandham’ or ‘Konchum Salangai Oli Kettu’. Thereafter a voice would say that just like Leela’s songs, the Leela calendar too was the best or that like a song by Leela, the namesake calendar too gave pleasure. This made an indelible impression on me that Leela was the best singer at that impressionable age. Of course in later years one listened to more old songs and realised that all those nightingales of yesteryear were unique in their own way.

My mother too was an ardent fan of Leela. Her favourite Tamil film singer duo or ‘jodi’ were Leela and the renowned singer Ghantasala. Leela was a Malayalee and Ghantasala a Telugu. Yet they were a popular pair singing in Tamil  and Telugu films then. Voice wise, Ghantasala was a bass and Leela a soprano but both harmonised well. My mother would often say that the voices of Ghantasala and Leela were made for each other. The duo hit it off perfectly in films like ‘Bhathala Bhairavi,’ ‘Maya Bhajaar’ and ‘Prema Pasam,’ with evergreen numbers like ‘Amaithi Illathen Maname,’ ‘Ennathan un Premaiyo,’ ‘Kathale Deiveega,’ ‘Neethanaa Ennai,’ ‘Aahaa Inba Nilavinile’ and ‘Veesum Thendralile,’ etc.

Apart from Ghantasala, Leela’s voice blended well with male singers like A.M. Rajah, T.M. Soundararajan and Seergali Govindarajan also.Among her many duets with AM Raja were ”Vaarayo Vennilaave” in ”Missiamma”, “odameethirunthe” in “Assai Magan” and “Naan seitha Poojapalam” in “Gunasundari”. Some of her memorable numbers with Soundararajan were “Mugathinil Mugam Parkalam ” (Thangappadumai), ” Nenjil kudiyirukkum” (Irumbuthirai), “Thendral urangidakkoodumadi” (Sangili Thevan), “Thalaiyam Poomudichu” (Bhagapirivinai) , “Sundari Soundari” (Thookku thookki), “Igalogame” (Thangamalai Rahasyam) “Poovindri Manamethu” (Thangappathumai) and “Nilave Nee Intha ” (Pattinathar).Seergali and Leela also hit it off well with items like “Ellaiyillatha Inbathile” (Chakkaravarthi Thirumagal) , “Kuttrala Aruviyile” (Nallavan Valvan) and “Kalathai Matrinan” (Manithan Maravillai).

As stated earlier when I was growing up – on a diet of Tamil films and Tamil film songs – playback singers like Ghantasala, Leela, A.M. Rajah, Jikki, Chidambaram Jeyaraman, M.L. Vasanthakumari, Tiruchi Loganathan, etc., were on the decline in Tamil films. This was not due to any shortcoming on their part. In fact they were at their peak then. Unfortunately they were not given many opportunities to sing in Tamil movies.

This was due to four reasons. One was that particular film music composers and film directors had begun avoiding them for various reasons. Another was that the old custom of having a variety of singers render playback was giving way to a practice of having only two or three singers per film. Thirdly the number of songs in a film too was being progressively reduced. Finally, leading actors and actresses of that time began insisting that only T.M. Soundararajan and P. Susheela should sing for them.

As a result, singers like Leela got only a few chances in Tamil after the early sixties. So the new Tamil films that I saw in those years did not have her songs. But there was always ‘Radio Ceylon’. I also became an ardent film aficionado and sought out old Tamil movies. Leela’s songs were featured in many of those.

Leela’s voice and singing style endeared her to me. It was an inimitable yet sweet amalgam of at least five of her contemporaries – M.L. Vasanthakumari, Jikki, (Radha) Jeyalakshmi, T.S. Bhagavathy and A.P. Komala. It was like a veritable “Panchamirtham” (an Ambrosiac mixture of milk, honey, curd, ghee and fruit juice offered as “prasadam” to the Gods). Leela’s resonant voice always rang out loud and clear whether in a solo, a duet or as part of chorus singing (Koshti Ganam).

Porayathu Leela was born in Chithoor near Palghat, Kerala on May 19 1934. Her early years were in Kochi where her father Kunchan Menon was a school master. He was devoted to Carnatic music, even encouraging Leela’s mother Meenakshi Kutti to learn Carnatic after marriage. The father recognised the musical ability and voice range of his daughter at a very early age. He arranged for music lessons and as a child Leela was made to practice alone between 4-6 a.m. daily.

Chennai or Madras was the South Indian capital during the Madras presidency days of the British Raj. It was the cultural Mecca for those of Dravidian linguistic heritage. Realising his daughter’s potential needed a change of place, Kunchan Menon resigned his job in 1944 and re-located to Madras. Leela continued her music tutelage under Rama Bhagavathar along with Pathamadai Krishna Aiyar and Maruthuvakkudi Rajagopal Aiyer in Madras. The father and daughter were also present for all Carnatic song events by reputed exponents.

Leela made her mark when she came first at the ‘Ragam-Thalam-Pallavi’ competition organised by the Madras Viswath Sabha in 1944. The judges were G.N. Balasubramaniam and Palghat Mani Aiyer. This gave her a break when the Andhra Mahila Samithi arranged for regular performances. She began making a name as an up-and-coming Carnatic musician. Neither the father nor daughter ever wanted to sing in films at that time.

The forties however saw a new phenomenon known as playback singing in Indian cinema. Instead of actors singing their songs they were merely lip-synching the words on screen. Regular singers were singing the songs. The final sound track was made after combining both through re-recording. Many professional singers were earning money and fame by singing in films.

The music composer Pathmanabha Shastri heard Leela sing and was impressed. An offer was made. Father and daughter were reluctant first. They were sceptical about the new medium. Finally they agreed. Leela made her entry into playback singing for cinema in 1948. It was the Tamil film ‘Kankanam’ starring K.R. Ramasamy and Menaka. Leela sat on the floor and sang into the mike. Four of her songs were recorded for the film. She never looked back after that.

1949 marked the turning point in her career. She began music lessons under musical prodigy C.R. Subbaraman (CRS), who was also scoring music for films. CRS gave chances to his pupil. Leela sang first for CRS in the Kannada film ‘Bhaktha Kabeer’ in 1949. CRS also gave opportunities to Leela in the Tamil movies ‘Mohini’ and ‘Velaikkari’. ‘Aahaa Ivar Yaaradi’ sung for ‘Mohini’ with K.V. Janaki and ‘Oridanthanile’ for ‘Velaikkari’ were hits. In the 1950s Leela began to sing in all South Indian films. A singing star was born.

It was also Subbaraman who enabled Leela to impress connoisseurs and commoners alike with a classic number. It was for the film ‘Manamagal’ and a duet for a dance by the ‘Travancore sisters,’ Lalitha and Padmini. CRS wanted Leela to sing with the legendary singer M.L. Vasanthakumari. Leela was nervous to sing along but was persuaded by her mentor. It was the ragamalika (medley) ‘Ellam Inba Mayam’.

MLV and Leela moved effortlessly from raga to raga off-screen even as Lalitha and Padmini danced in rhythmic movement on-screen. Leela held her own with MLV and established her name as a singer par excellence of classical melody. Incidentally MLV and Leela were the only two playback singers of that generation with formal training in Carnatic music.

Leela was a singer who could sing almost any type of song at any pace. Classical, semi classical, light and folk songs were all her forte. Devotional, dancing, gypsy, folk, romantic songs were all part of her repertoire. She could grasp the melody easily and sing with all intonations. She kept the ‘shruthi’ low and sang out lustily, giving full range to her mellifluous voice.

Leela was a simple and humble person without any airs or tantrums. She cooperated with all her music directors and co-singers well. Leela has sung for many actresses like Padmini, Savithri, Jamuna, Anjali Devi, T.R. Rajakumari, Rajasulosana, Vijayakumari and even Saroja Devi. It is felt that her voice suited Padmini and Savithri best. She has also sung for composers like C.R. Subbaraman, Ghantasala, K.V. Mahadevan, S.M. Subbiah Naidu, Rajeswara Rao, C. Ramachandra, G. Ramanathan and Viswanathan-Ramamoorthy. Almost every composer elicited the best from her, though CRS, Ghantasala, KVM and G. Ramanathan excelled in utilising her talent.
There are innumerable solos in Tamil films by Leela. In my opinion the finest four of her solos were in ‘Missiamma,’ ‘Vanchikottai Vaaliban’ and ‘Thangappathumai’.

‘Missiamma’ starred Gemini Ganesh and real-life wife Savithri. The two remarkable Leela solos were ‘Mayame Naan Ariven’ and ‘Enaiyalum Mary Matha’. The latter an appeal to the Virgin Mary was sung on an emotional high though Leela is a devout Hindu. It is on par with the Jikki-Periyanayaki number on Mary in ‘Gnanasoundari’ – ‘Arul Tharum Theva Mathave’.

The solo from ‘Vanchikottai Valiban’ is ‘Vennilave Thanmathiye Ennudane Va Va’. It is a soothing melody set on screen for Padmini in a nocturnal boat scene. The melody is evocative of the gentle breeze, moonlight glow, ripples in the water and above all the slow, rhythmic movement of the boat. It transports one to an ethereal atmosphere.

The fourth solo is the sad number ‘Sollamma Vai Thiranthu’ in ‘Thangappathumai’. It is played out by Padmini on screen. The music is by Viswanathan-Ramamoorthy. Leela has gone on record saying that it was the most difficult song she has ever sung for Thamizh films. The recording took place from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. at a 12-hour stretch. Since it was full of pathos, Leela was emotionally high-strung. Viswanathan was not easily satisfied and made Leela labour ceaselessly. At the end of it all Leela wept uncontrollably. Leela’s singing plus Padmini’s acting made the song sequence a great success.

Even if I consider the above four as the four best solos of Leela, the one that I like most  of her solos is the song ‘Then Suvai Mevum Senthamil Geetham’ in ‘Dr. Savithri’. The musical score is by maestro G. Ramanathan, who usually gave ample scope to Leela in the movies he worked in. The song is based on Eastern and Western classical music. The song is picturised on actress Anjali Devi and “Veena”maestro S. Balachander who plays the piano himself in the scene.

Leela sings in sopranic tones to piano accompaniment. The piano interludes are slight variations of a classical movement. Leela begins with an ‘Aaahaara bhirugha’ of incredible sweetness and follows up with similar interspersals. The words too extol the virtues of music and song. She sings ‘Aanandha Geetham’ (A Song of Happiness) is an “Amudha Saagaram’ (Ocean of Ambrosia). How true! The song is almost like a mission statement signed by Leela.

Among other popular and well – appreciated solos are “Kathiruppan Kamalakkannan” in “Uthama Puthiran” where Leela sings a ragamalika set by Ramanathan for a Bharatham sequence by Padmini and her younger sibling Ragini. Leela moves from one raga to another smoothly. So remarkable is the singing that many mistake the singer for MLV. Another of Leelas clasical gems is “Neeye Kathi Eeswari”set to Sarukesi raga in “Annayin Aanai”. The composer is SM Subbaiah Naidu who also appreciated Leela’s work greatly.

It was Subbiah who composed music for “Konchum Salangai” for which Leela sings the theme song “Konchum Salangai Oli Kettu, Nenjil Ponguthamma Puthiya Paattu”. The hit song of the film however was “Singara Velane Deva”. It was this song – accompanied by Karukurichi Arunasalam’s nathaswaram – that launched S. Janaki’s career. What is interesting is that Subbiah Naidu wanted Leela to sing it initially.

Leela however declined saying she could not do full justice to the song and recommended the relatively unknown Janaki as most suitable to sing it. A rare act of frankness and magnanimity!. When Janaki performed in Toronto some years ago, she recollected this incident and expressed her gratitude to” Leelaamma.”

Leela has also sung several duets with singers of her own sex. The songstress combinations have been successes very often. It was the “inbamayam” duet with MLV in “Manamagal” that made Leela’s name. There have been other duets with others too like “Vetkamaga irukkuthadi” – Soolamangalam Rajalakshmi in “Paar Magakey Paar” and “Vaaliya Needooli” – (Radha) Jayalakshmi in “Arivaali”. Both were bharatanatyam numbers. Leela however clicked marvellously with Jikki and P. Susheela.

The crowning achievement with Jikki was in “Vanchikottai Vaaliban” by Gemini Studios. Music was by C. Ramachandra of “Anarkali” fame. Two great danseuse actresses Padmini and Vyjayanthimala were the heroines vying for hero Gemini Ganesans affection. There is this magnificient dance competition between both in the presence of the hero and villain Veerappa. Jikki sings for Vyjayanthi and Leela for Padmini in the classic number “Kannum Kannum Kalanthu”. The song written by Kothamangalam Subbu is a verbal duel. The words, melody, dancing and singing prove to be an excellent combination. At one point Veerappa exclaims “Sabash! Sariyana Potti” (wow! Great competition). It was not only apt for the on screen dancing but also applicable for the off – screen singing by Jikki and Leela.

Leela’s much praised duet with Susheela was in “Lavakusha” starring NT Rama Rao and Anjali Devi. The duet was a marathon 12 minute number “Jegam Pugalum Punniya Kathai Ramanin Kasthaiye, Athai Sevi kulira Padiduvom Kelungal Ithaiye”. The words of Maruthakasi encapsule the story of Ramayana in that song. The music is by Ghantasala. Leela and Susheela sing it in Telugu also in the Telugu version of the film.Sri Sathya Sai Baba was greatly enamoured of that song. He referred to Leela and Susheela as Lava and Kusha because it was Lava and Kusa who sing it on screen.
It was indeed a pity that a songstress of Leela’s calibre was slowly sent into oblivion by Tamil cinema. Leela was not one to canvass for songs and allowed herself to fade away gracefully.

Leela sang in a Tamil film in 1970 after a gap of many years. The film was ‘Engirundho Vanthal,’ where a skit on Sakunthalai was performed on screen. The song by Seergali Govindarajan and Leela begins as ‘Kalithasa Maha Kavi Kaviyam, Kanni Sakunthalai Endroru Oviyam’. It was poignantly appropriate that Leela voiced for Sakunthalai. Like Dushyanthan in Kalidas’s epic forgetting Sakunthalai, the Tamil film world too had forgotten Leela.

After ‘Engiruntho Vandhal’ in 1970 there was a long lull till Ilaiyarajah got her to sing in ‘Katpoora Mullai’ in 1984. It was for a classical song by Sri Vidya on screen. The actress was the daughter of M.L. Vasanthakumari with whom Leela has sung together in many films.

Apart from Tamil cinema even the orthodox music societies of Tamil Nadu ignored Leela. She was a formally trained Carnatic singer but the ‘Sabhas’ of Chennai did not give her many assignments, treating her as a second class playback singer. Kerala and Andhra gave her many stage programs. She was however given continuous prominence by Malayalam radio and television.

Leela’s marriage to a lawyer was a failure. She lived with her sister and family in a Chennai suburb until her demise in 2005 at the age of 71. Former film star Jayalalitha Jayaram who was then Tamil Nadu Chief Minister summed up Leela’s life aptly in her condolence message. Jayalalitha described Leela as “one of the greatest vocal musicians of India, who made a name for herself by singing exquisite songs in her mellifluous voice in Malayalam, Tamil and Telugu both for the film industry, as well as in Carnatic music.”

The Tamil tinsel world and snobbish Sabhas may have rejected Leela, but thousands of her fans never spurned her. Whenever a program featuring her was staged in Tamil Nadu or abroad, many flocked to see and hear her. Unfortunately, such occasions were few. Technology however enables her fans to listen again and again to the melodious voice at a mere click.
Tamil cinema may have forgotten Leela, but her songs remain unforgettable. The name P. Leela can never be erased from the sphere of Tamil film songs.
